simple: The boss gave him a real dressing-down.

chinese: 反覆灌輸 / 鑽孔進入
example: Our coach drilled the importance of teamwork into us every single day.
example_cn: 我們的教練每天都不斷灌輸我們團隊合作的重要性。
simple: The carpenter drilled a hole into the wood.

example: They stood on the mountain peak, drinking in the breathtaking view of the valley.
example_cn: 他們站在山頂上，盡情欣賞山谷的壯麗景色。
simple: The tourists drank in the atmosphere of the market.

example: Drink up your orange juice so we can leave for school.
example_cn: 把柳橙汁喝完，我們才能出發去學校。
simple: Drink up! There's plenty more.

example: I’m not quite sure what you are driving at with those strange comments.
example_cn: 我不太確定你那些奇怪的發言到底想表達什麼。
simple: I don't understand what he is driving at.

example: The loud noise of the alarm drove away the burglars.
example_cn: 警報器的巨大聲響把小偷嚇跑了。
simple: The soldiers managed to drive back the enemy.

example: The professor droned on for over an hour, and most of the students fell asleep.
example_cn: 教授嘮叨了一個多小時，大部分學生都睡著了。
simple: He droned on about his old car.

example: All the fashion fans were drooling over the new designer collection.
example_cn: 所有時尚迷都對新設計師系列垂涎三尺。
simple: He spent all afternoon drooling over sports cars.

example: The runner began to drop behind the rest of the group in the final stage of the race.
example_cn: 那位跑者在比賽最後階段開始落後於其他人。
simple: Sales began to drop away last month.

example: The noise of the passing train drowned out our conversation.
example_cn: 火車經過的噪音蓋過了我們的談話聲。
simple: Her voice was drowned out by the wind.

example: My parents drummed the importance of honesty into me from a young age.
example_cn: 我父母從小就灌輸我要誠實的重要性。
simple: The coach drummed discipline into the players.

chinese: 招徠 / 爭取 (支持/生意)
example: The company is launching a new advertising campaign to drum up more business.
example_cn: 公司正在推出新的廣告活動，希望能招攬更多生意。
simple: He is trying to drum up support for the new policy.

chinese: 擦乾 / 乾涸 / 枯萎
example: The small river completely dried up during the long summer drought.
example_cn: 那條小河在漫長的夏季乾旱期間完全乾涸了。
simple: Use a towel to dry yourself off.

example: He tried to duck out of the meeting early, but his boss saw him leaving.
example_cn: 他試圖提早溜出會議，但被老闆看到他在離開。
simple: You can't duck out of your responsibilities.

chinese: 老是想著 (不快的事) / 詳述
example: You shouldn't dwell on your past mistakes; focus on your future instead.
example_cn: 你不該一直糾結過去的錯誤，而是要專注在未來。
simple: He dwelt on the negative aspects of the plan.

chinese: 減輕 / 緩放 / 放鬆
example: The heavy rain finally began to ease off around midnight.
example_cn: 大雨終於在午夜左右開始緩和下來。
simple: Ease up on the accelerator!

chinese: 腐蝕 / 侵蝕 / 消耗
example: The acidic rain slowly eats away at the ancient stone statues in the park.
example_cn: 酸雨慢慢侵蝕公園裡那些古老的石像。
simple: Guilt began to eat away at his mind.

chinese: 消耗 (金錢、時間) / 侵蝕
example: The high cost of living in the city is starting to eat into our monthly savings.
example_cn: 城市裡的高生活費開始吃掉我們每月的積蓄。
simple: Responsibilities eat into my free time.

chinese: 充滿...的聲音 / 迴響著
example: The empty hallways echoed with the sound of the children's laughter.
example_cn: 空蕩的走廊迴盪著孩子們的笑聲。
simple: The stadium echoed with cheers.

chinese: 擠掉 / 以微弱優勢擊敗
example: The small local shop was eventually edged out by the large supermarket chain.
example_cn: 那家當地小商店最終被大型連鎖超市擠掉了。
pattern: S + be edged out by + N
simple: He was edged out of the starting lineup.

chinese: 艱苦維持 / 勉強彌補 (不足)
example: The refugees managed to eke out a living in the desert despite the harsh conditions.
example_cn: 儘管環境惡劣，那些難民還是在沙漠中勉強維持生計。
simple: They eke out a meager existence on a small farm.

example: Could you please elaborate on your proposal so we can understand the details?
example_cn: 可以請你詳細說明你的提案嗎？這樣我們才能了解細節。
simple: He refused to elaborate on his plans.

example: The team was eliminated from the competition after losing the first round.
example_cn: 那支隊伍在第一輪輸掉後，就被淘汰出比賽了。
simple: Try to eliminate fat from your diet.

chinese: 著手進行 / 開始 (新旅程或計畫)
example: The young entrepreneur is ready to embark on a new business venture.
example_cn: 那位年輕的企業家準備好要展開一項新的事業。
simple: She embarked on a career in politics.

chinese: 從...中出現 / 擺脫 (困境)
example: The bear slowly emerged from its cave after a long winter of hibernation.
example_cn: 那隻熊在漫長的冬眠後，慢慢從洞穴裡走了出來。
simple: The country is emerging from a recession.

example: The Mississippi River eventually empties into the Gulf of Mexico.
example_cn: 密西西比河最終流入墨西哥灣。
pattern: 河流 + empty(ies) into + 海/湖
simple: All these pipes empty into the main sewer.

example: The scholarship enabled her to study abroad without worrying about the cost.
example_cn: 這筆獎學金讓她能夠出國留學，不用擔心費用問題。
simple: The new technology enables us to work faster.

example: Parents should encourage their children in their creative pursuits.
example_cn: 父母應該鼓勵孩子從事他們的創意興趣。
simple: She encouraged me in my ambition.

example: She was endowed with a beautiful singing voice and natural acting talent.
example_cn: 她天生擁有一副好歌喉和自然的演戲天賦。
simple: Humans are endowed with reason.

example: The two countries decided to engage in diplomatic talks to resolve the conflict.
example_cn: 這兩個國家決定進行外交談判來解決衝突。
simple: He engages in regular exercise.

example: My grandmother always enquires after you whenever I visit her.
example_cn: 我每次去看我阿嬤，她都會問起你。
simple: He called to enquire after his sick friend.

example: A committee has been formed to enquire into the causes of the accident.
example_cn: 已經成立了一個委員會，要調查這起事故的原因。
simple: The police are enquiring into the robbery.

example: She decided to enrol in an online photography course to improve her skills.
example_cn: 她決定報名線上攝影課程，來提升自己的技巧。
simple: They enrolled their child in a private school.

chinese: 因...而產生 / 隨之而來
example: The problems that ensued from the merger were more difficult than expected.
example_cn: 合併後隨之而來的問題，比預期的還要棘手。
simple: Chaos ensued from the power failure.

chinese: 防範 / 確保不發生 (負面事物)
example: We need to update our security system to ensure against cyber attacks.
example_cn: 我們需要更新安全系統，以防範網路攻擊。
simple: The policy ensures against future losses.

example: The young athlete decided to enter for the marathon next spring.
example_cn: 那位年輕運動員決定報名參加明年春天的馬拉松。
simple: She entered for the entrance exam.

chinese: 進入 (討論) / 締結 (協議)
example: The two companies are planning to enter into a partnership next year.
example_cn: 這兩家公司計畫明年要建立合作關係。
simple: They entered into a long argument.

example: This membership card entitles you to a ten percent discount at all our stores.
example_cn: 持有這張會員卡，可以在我們所有門市享有九折優惠。
simple: You are entitled to your opinion.

example: The manager entrusted the important task to his most reliable assistant.
example_cn: 經理把這項重要任務，交給了他最信賴的助理。
simple: I entrusted my keys to the neighbor.

example: It is difficult to erase painful memories from one's mind completely.
example_cn: 要完全抹去心裡的痛苦回憶，是很困難的。
simple: She erased the name from the list.

chinese: 由...演化而成 / 發展成為
example: What started as a simple idea eventually evolved into a global movement.
example_cn: 原本只是一個簡單的想法，最後卻發展成一場全球性的運動。
simple: Birds are thought to have evolved from dinosaurs.

example: She has always excelled in mathematics and science since she was a primary school student.
example_cn: 她從小學開始，數學和理科就一直很出色。
simple: He excels at playing the piano.

type: 片語動詞 / 形容詞片語
example: Low-income families are often exempt from paying certain local taxes.
example_cn: 低收入家庭通常可以免繳某些地方稅。
simple: The charity is exempt from tax.

example: The speaker asked for more time to expand on the results of the research.
example_cn: 講者要求多一點時間，來詳細說明這項研究的結果。
simple: Could you expand on that idea?

example: The manager expects a high level of dedication from all of his employees.
example_cn: 經理期望他所有的員工都能有高度的投入。
simple: I expect better of you.

chinese: 對...做實驗 / 嘗試 (新方法)
example: Scientists are currently experimenting with new materials to create lighter batteries.
example_cn: 科學家們正在實驗用新材料來製造更輕的電池。
simple: They experimented on lab mice.

example: The doctor spent a long time explaining the treatment to the concerned parents.
example_cn: 醫生花了很長時間，向擔心的家長解釋治療方式。
simple: Can you explain this rule to me?

chinese: 使暴露於... / 使接觸到
example: Traveling can expose you to different ways of thinking and living.
example_cn: 旅行可以讓你接觸到不同的思考與生活方式。
simple: The painting was exposed to direct sunlight.

chinese: 延伸到 / 給予 (邀請、感激)
example: The invitation was extended to all members of the local community.
example_cn: 這份邀請函發給了當地社區的所有成員。
simple: The deadline was extended to next week.

example: Scientists can extract DNA from very small samples of hair or skin.
example_cn: 科學家可以從非常少量的毛髮或皮膚樣本中，提取出DNA。
pattern: S + extract + N + from + 來源
simple: They managed to extract a confession from him.

chinese: 勇敢地面對並壓倒 (對手)
example: The politician managed to face down his critics and win the vote.
example_cn: 那位政治人物成功壓制了批評聲浪，贏得了投票。
simple: The protesters faced down the police.

example: His anger slowly faded away as he realized it was just a misunderstanding.
example_cn: 他發現只是誤會一場後，怒氣就慢慢消退了。
simple: The music faded away into the distance.

example: If you fail in your duties, the entire team will suffer the consequences.
example_cn: 如果你沒盡到責任，整個團隊都得承擔後果。
simple: He failed in his attempt to break the record.

example: When his plan failed, he had no savings to fall back on.
example_cn: 他的計畫失敗時，根本沒有積蓄可以依靠。
pattern: S + have + N + to fall back on
simple: She can always fall back on her medical training.

chinese: 落空 / 失敗 (計畫、協議)
example: The deal fell through at the last minute because the two companies couldn't agree on the price.
example_cn: 這筆交易在最後一刻告吹，因為兩家公司無法在價格上達成共識。
simple: The trip fell through when she got sick.

example: You should familiarize yourself with the rules before you start the game.
example_cn: 你應該在開始遊戲前先熟悉規則。
simple: Please familiarize yourself with the safety exit.

chinese: 抵擋 / 避開 (問題、攻擊)
example: The manager had to fend off a series of difficult questions during the press conference.
example_cn: 經理在記者會上不得不應付一連串尖銳的問題。
simple: He used his umbrella to fend off the dog.

example: It's better to fess up now than to wait until they catch you.
example_cn: 與其等到被逮到，不如現在就老實招認。
simple: Come on, fess up! Who ate the cake?

example: The soldiers fought back bravely against the invaders.
example_cn: 士兵們奮勇反擊入侵者。
simple: She tried to fight back her tears.

example: My body is trying to fight off a cold, so I feel a bit tired today.
example_cn: 我的身體正在努力對抗感冒，所以今天覺得有點累。
simple: They fought off the attackers.

example: Did you figure in the cost of shipping when you set the price?
example_cn: 你定價時有把運費算進去嗎？
simple: The inflation rate must be figured in.

example: The company was forced to file for bankruptcy after failing to pay its debts.
example_cn: 那家公司因為無力償還債務，被迫申請破產。
simple: They filed for divorce after two years.

example: We need to stop at a gas station to fill up the tank before we head out.
example_cn: 我們出發前得先去加油站把油箱加滿。
simple: The stadium filled up quickly.

chinese: 過濾掉 / (消息)走漏 / 透過
example: The sunlight filtered through the dense leaves of the forest.
example_cn: 陽光穿過森林茂密的樹葉灑落下來。
simple: The air filter filters out dust.

chinese: 完成 / 結束 / 吃完
example: Let's finish off the remaining work so we can go home early.
example_cn: 我們把剩下的工作收尾，這樣就能早點回家了。
simple: They finished up the pizza.

example: If you have any questions about the project, just fire away!
example_cn: 如果你對這個專案有任何問題，儘管問吧！
simple: I'm ready to answer, so fire away.

chinese: 快速發出 (郵件、問題) / 砲轟
example: The angry customer fired off a series of emails to the company's head office.
example_cn: 那位生氣的顧客接連發了好幾封郵件到公司總部。
simple: The reporter fired off three questions at once.

chinese: 啟動 (引擎、電腦) / 激起熱情
example: We need to fire up the old generator before the storm gets any worse.
example_cn: 我們得在暴風雨變得更糟之前，把那台舊發電機啟動起來。
simple: The coach's speech really fired up the players.

chinese: 使穩固 / 確定 (計畫、細節)
example: We need to meet tomorrow to firm up the details of our travel itinerary.
example_cn: 我們明天得開會把旅遊行程的細節敲定。
simple: The company is trying to firm up its position in the market.

chinese: 間接打聽 / 渴望獲得 (讚美、資訊)
example: She was obviously fishing for a compliment when she showed us her new painting.
example_cn: 她給我們看她的新畫作時，明顯是在討稱讚。
simple: He was fishing for information about the secret plan.

chinese: 適應 / 融合 / 抽空安排
example: The new student struggled to fit in with her classmates at first.
example_cn: 那個新來的學生一開始很難融入班上同學。
simple: The doctor can fit you in at four o'clock.

chinese: 逐漸消失 / 虎頭蛇尾
example: The initial enthusiasm for the project slowly fizzled out as the weeks passed.
example_cn: 對這個計畫的初期熱情，隨著時間過去慢慢就淡掉了。
simple: Their relationship just fizzled out after they moved away.

example: He managed to flag down a passing police car after his car broke down on the highway.
example_cn: 他的車在高速公路上拋錨後，他設法攔下了一輛路過的警車。
simple: We flagged down a taxi near the station.

chinese: (火焰)突然燃起 / (情緒、病痛)突然爆發
example: Tensions between the two countries flared up again over the border dispute.
example_cn: 兩國之間因為邊界爭議，緊張局勢又再度升溫。
simple: His back pain flared up during the long flight.

example: The movie frequently flashes back to the main character's childhood in the countryside.
example_cn: 這部電影經常倒敘主角在鄉下的童年時光。
simple: My mind flashed back to the day of the accident.

chinese: 充實內容 / 使...具體化
example: You have a good idea, but you need to flesh it out with more specific details.
example_cn: 你的點子不錯，但需要加入更具體的細節來把它充實起來。
simple: The writer fleshed out the characters in the second chapter.

chinese: 快速翻閱 / 瀏覽 (電視頻道)
example: I was flicking through a magazine while waiting for my appointment at the clinic.
example_cn: 我在診所等看診的時候，隨手翻了一下雜誌。
simple: He spent the evening flicking through TV channels.

chinese: 抓狂 / 發瘋 / 激動
example: My mother will flip out when she sees the mess we made in the kitchen.
example_cn: 我媽看到我們把廚房弄得亂七八糟，一定會抓狂。
simple: I nearly flipped out when I heard I won the lottery.

chinese: 調情 / 挑逗 / 考慮 (某主意) / 冒...風險
example: The company is flirting with the idea of moving its headquarters to Singapore.
example_cn: 這間公司正在考慮要不要把總部搬到新加坡。
simple: Stop flirting with my sister!

example: Applications began to flood in as soon as the job advertisement was posted online.
example_cn: 徵才廣告一貼上網，求職申請就開始大量湧入。
simple: Letters of support flooded in from all over the country.

example: The company's problems flow from a lack of effective leadership at the top.
example_cn: 這間公司的問題，源於高層缺乏有效的領導。
simple: Wealth does not always flow from hard work.

example: He spent too much time playing games and eventually flunked out of university.
example_cn: 他花太多時間打電動，最後被大學退學了。
simple: He flunked out in his first semester.

example: The police used tear gas to flush the suspects out of the abandoned building.
example_cn: 警方使用催淚瓦斯，把嫌犯從廢棄建築物裡逼出來。
simple: Drink plenty of water to flush out the toxins.

chinese: 貫徹始終 / 完成 (動作)
example: The government promised to reduce taxes, but they failed to follow through on their promise.
example_cn: 政府承諾要減稅，但最後卻沒有兌現承諾。
simple: You need to follow through with your training plan.

chinese: 後續跟進 / 進一步行動
example: The doctor will follow up with another appointment in two weeks to check your progress.
example_cn: 醫生會在兩週後安排回診，追蹤你的恢復情況。
simple: I need to follow up on that lead.

chinese: 閒逛 / 胡鬧 / 浪費時間
example: Stop fooling around and start studying for your final exams!
example_cn: 別再鬼混了，趕快開始準備期末考！
simple: The kids were just fooling around in the pool.

example: In the winter, wild animals often have to forage for food under the snow.
example_cn: 冬天時，野生動物常常得在雪地下方尋找食物。
simple: We spent the afternoon foraging for mushrooms.

chinese: 擊退 / 抑制 (眼淚、情緒)
example: The soldiers fought bravely to force back the invading army.
example_cn: 士兵們英勇作戰，成功擊退了入侵的軍隊。
simple: She tried to force back her tears.

example: I need to delete some old photos to free up some space on my smartphone.
example_cn: 我需要刪掉一些舊照片，來釋放手機的儲存空間。
simple: The new law will free up more money for education.

example: The lake usually freezes over in January, allowing people to go ice skating.
example_cn: 這個湖通常在一月會結冰，讓人們可以去溜冰。
simple: The river has frozen over.

example: My computer keeps freezing up whenever I try to open this large file.
example_cn: 我的電腦每次要開這個大檔案就會一直當機。
simple: I froze up when the teacher called my name.

example: I need to freshen up before we head out for dinner; it's been a long flight.
example_cn: 出門吃晚餐前我得先梳洗一下，這趟飛行實在太久了。
simple: The room needs a coat of paint to freshen it up.

example: Don't fret about the results of the test; you've already done your best.
example_cn: 別太擔心考試的結果，你已經盡力了。
simple: There's no point fretting about things you can't change.

example: The barking of the dog frightened away the stranger at the gate.
example_cn: 狗的叫聲把門口那個陌生人嚇跑了。
simple: The high prices frightened off many customers.

example: Smoking in the office is strictly frowned upon by the company management.
example_cn: 公司管理階層非常不贊同在辦公室抽菸。
simple: Public displays of anger are frowned on here.

example: We should stop and fuel up before we start our long drive across the desert.
example_cn: 在我們開始穿越沙漠的長途駕駛之前，應該先停下來加油。
simple: The plane is fueling up for the flight.

chinese: 過分關心 / 對...大驚小怪
example: My grandmother always fusses over me whenever I visit her, making sure I eat enough.
example_cn: 我奶奶每次我去看她，總會一直嘮叨，確保我吃飽。
simple: She fusses over every small detail.

example: The young singer has gained in confidence since her first public performance.
example_cn: 那位年輕歌手自從第一次公開演出後，變得更有自信了。
simple: The house has gained in value.

chinese: (因賭博) 輸掉 / 揮霍掉
example: He sadly gambled away his entire inheritance in just a few months.
example_cn: 他難過地在短短幾個月內就把整筆遺產賭光了。
simple: Don't gamble your future away.

chinese: 收拾 / 收集 / 鼓起 (勇氣)
example: The children gathered up their toys and went inside for dinner.
example_cn: 孩子們把玩具收好，然後進屋裡吃晚餐。
simple: She gathered up her courage to speak.

example: They sat on the beach and gazed at the beautiful sunset over the ocean.
example_cn: 他們坐在沙灘上，凝視著海面上美麗的夕陽。
simple: He gazed at her in wonder.

chinese: 為...做好準備 / 蓄勢待發
example: The tech company is gearing up for the launch of its new smartphone next month.
example_cn: 這家科技公司正在為下個月新款智慧型手機的上市做準備。
simple: Schools are gearing up for the new semester.

chinese: 解釋清楚 / 使人理解
example: The teacher used simple diagrams to help get the complicated concept across.
example_cn: 老師用簡單的圖表來幫助大家理解這個複雜的概念。
simple: I failed to get my point across.

example: He worked late every night to get ahead of his colleagues in the department.
example_cn: 他每晚都工作到很晚，就是為了在部門裡超越同事。
simple: The car speeded up to get ahead of the bus.

example: She played a prank on him just to get back at him for what he did earlier.
example_cn: 她對他惡作劇，只是想報復他之前做的事。
pattern: S + get back at + 人 (for + N)
simple: I'll get back at him one day.

chinese: 回覆 / 回到 (主題/工作)
example: I'm not sure of the answer right now, but I will get back to you by tomorrow.
example_cn: 我現在不確定答案，但我明天之前會再回覆你。
simple: Let's get back to work.

example: If you don't submit your reports on time, you will quickly get behind with your work.
example_cn: 如果你不按時交報告，很快就會在工作進度上落後。
simple: The whole community got behind the new project.

example: He was determined to get even with his neighbor for cutting down the old tree.
example_cn: 他決心要報復鄰居砍掉那棵老樹。
simple: I'll get even with you for this!

chinese: 出發 / 開始 / 運作起來
example: It's getting late; we should really get going if we want to catch the last train.
example_cn: 時間不早了，我們真的該出發了，才能趕上最後一班火車。
simple: The project is finally getting going.

chinese: 與...相處融洽 / 繼續做 (某事)
example: I get on very well with my new classmates, and we often study together.
example_cn: 我跟新同學相處得很好，我們經常一起唸書。
pattern: S + get on with + 人/物
simple: Stop talking and get on with your work.

chinese: 抽空去做 / 終於著手做 (延宕已久的事)
example: I haven't got round to reading that book yet, but I plan to do it during the holiday.
example_cn: 我還沒空讀那本書，但打算趁假期來讀。
simple: I finally got round to fixing the faucet.

chinese: 讓路給 / 被...取代 / 屈服於
example: Drivers must give way to pedestrians at the zebra crossing.
example_cn: 駕駛人在斑馬線前必須禮讓行人。
simple: Traditional shops are giving way to modern malls.

example: She glanced through the menu quickly and ordered a salad.
example_cn: 她快速瀏覽了一下菜單，然後點了一份沙拉。
simple: He glanced through the report before the meeting.

example: Her eyes began to glaze over as the professor continued his long and boring lecture.
example_cn: 教授繼續他那又長又無聊的課，她的眼神開始變得呆滯。
simple: His eyes glazed over when I mentioned math.

chinese: 掩蓋 (錯誤、缺陷) / 帶過
example: The report glossed over the company's financial problems to make the situation look better.
example_cn: 那份報告輕描淡寫了公司的財務問題，讓情況看起來比較好。
simple: Don't try to gloss over your mistakes.

example: The secret of what she had done began to gnaw at her conscience.
example_cn: 她所做的那件事的秘密，開始不斷啃噬她的良心。
simple: Fear gnawed at his heart.

chinese: 食言 / 違背 (諾言、決定)
example: You promised to help us, and it's too late to go back on your word now.
example_cn: 你答應過要幫我們，現在反悔已經太遲了。
simple: He would never go back on a promise.

chinese: 破產 / 倒閉 / 沉沒 / 接受麻醉
example: Thousands of small businesses went under during the global economic recession.
example_cn: 全球經濟衰退期間，好幾千家小型企業倒閉了。
simple: The ship went under in the heavy storm.

chinese: 做好準備 / 蓄勢待發
example: The factory is gearing up for a major increase in production next month.
example_cn: 工廠正在加緊準備，下個月要大幅增產。
simple: The team is gearing up for the finals.

example: The poet's latest work gives voice to the struggles of the working class.
example_cn: 這位詩人的最新作品，為工人階級的困境發聲。
simple: She gave voice to our fears.

chinese: 上升 / 增長 / (建築物)被興建
example: The price of gasoline is expected to go up again next week due to the global oil shortage.
example_cn: 由於全球石油短缺，預計下週汽油價格會再次上漲。
simple: New apartment blocks are going up all over the city.

chinese: 沒有...也行 / 將就 / 忍受缺乏
example: We had to go without fresh water for two days after the main pipe burst.
example_cn: 主水管爆裂後，我們有兩天沒新鮮水可用。
simple: He can't go without coffee in the morning.

chinese: 狼吞虎嚥 / 迅速消耗
example: The hungry children gobbled up the entire pizza in less than ten minutes.
example_cn: 那些餓壞的孩子們，不到十分鐘就把整塊披薩吃光了。
simple: High fuel prices are gobbling up our savings.

chinese: 狼吞虎嚥 / 塞滿 (食物)
example: It's easy to gorge on sweets and snacks while watching a long movie.
example_cn: 看長片的時候，很容易忍不住大吃糖果和零食。
simple: The guests gorged on the expensive buffet.

example: She graduated from National Taiwan University with a degree in political science.
example_cn: 她畢業於國立臺灣大學，主修政治學。
simple: He graduated from high school last June.

chinese: 扭打 / 努力解決 (難題)
example: The government is currently grappling with the problem of rising unemployment.
example_cn: 政府目前正在努力應對失業率攀升的問題。
simple: She is grappling with a difficult decision.

chinese: 磨擦 / 使人煩躁 / 刺耳
example: His constant complaining began to grate on my nerves after a few hours.
example_cn: 他不停抱怨，幾個小時後就開始讓我感到煩躁。
simple: The high-pitched sound grates on my ears.

example: Younger voters tend to gravitate toward candidates who promise environmental reform.
example_cn: 年輕選民往往傾向於支持承諾環保改革的候選人。
simple: Tourists gravitate to the city center.

chinese: (牲畜)吃草 / 吃零食 (代正餐)
example: We saw a small group of cows grazing on the grass in the field.
example_cn: 我們看到一小群牛在田野裡吃草。
simple: I prefer grazing on healthy snacks throughout the day.

chinese: 以...迎接 / 受到...的反應
example: The singer's performance was greeted with a standing ovation from the crowd.
example_cn: 那位歌手的表演獲得全場觀眾起立鼓掌。
simple: He greeted her with a warm smile.

chinese: 磨碎 / 壓垮 / 使精疲力竭
example: Years of hard physical labor eventually ground him down.
example_cn: 長年做粗重的工作，最後終於把他給拖垮了。
simple: The continuous stress is grinding her down.

chinese: 艱難地產出 / 粗製濫造
example: He had to grind out a 2,000-word essay by tomorrow morning.
example_cn: 他得在明天早上前硬擠出一篇兩千字的文章。
simple: The factory grinds out cheap toys every day.

example: Stop griping about the weather and enjoy your holiday!
example_cn: 別再抱怨天氣了，好好享受你的假期吧！
simple: Employees were griping about the low pay.

example: The table was groaning under the weight of all the delicious food.
example_cn: 那張桌子被滿滿的美食壓得都快垮了。
simple: The country is groaning under heavy taxes.

chinese: 訓練 / 培養...接替職務
example: She is being groomed for the position of CEO when the current one retires.
example_cn: 她正在被培養成下一任執行長，等現任的退休後就接棒。
simple: He was groomed for a career in law.

chinese: 摸索 / 搜尋 (字眼、解答)
example: He was so nervous during the interview that he had to grope for the right words.
example_cn: 他面試時太緊張了，只好邊講邊摸索適當的用詞。
simple: She groped for the light switch in the dark.

example: His theories are firmly grounded in scientific facts and evidence.
example_cn: 他的理論完全建立在科學事實與證據之上。
simple: The course provides students with a good grounding in math.

example: The two best friends slowly grew apart after they went to different universities.
example_cn: 那對最好的朋友上了不同大學後，感情就慢慢變淡了。
simple: Couples often grow apart over the years.

chinese: 漸漸喜歡上 / 逐漸加深印象
example: I didn't like the song at first, but it has really started to grow on me.
example_cn: 我一開始不喜歡這首歌，但現在真的越聽越順耳。
simple: The city will grow on you once you stay longer.

chinese: 長大而穿不下 / 源於 / 戒除 (習慣)
example: The little boy has already grown out of the shoes we bought him last year.
example_cn: 那個小男孩去年買給他的鞋子，現在已經穿不下了。
simple: He eventually grew out of his childhood fears.

example: Some people just love to grumble about the weather no matter how nice it is.
example_cn: 有些人就是愛抱怨天氣，不管天氣多好都一樣。
simple: The passengers grumbled about the delay.

chinese: 防範 / 預防 (負面事物)
example: We must install security software to guard against viruses and hackers.
example_cn: 我們必須安裝安全軟體，以防病毒和駭客入侵。
simple: Proper nutrition helps guard against illness.

example: Without accurate data, we can only guess at the real cause of the problem.
example_cn: 沒有準確的數據，我們只能猜測問題的真正原因。
pattern: S + can only guess at + N
simple: He could only guess at what she was thinking.

example: The teacher's role is to guide students to find their own answers.
example_cn: 老師的角色是引導學生自己找到答案。
simple: The stars guided them to the shore.

chinese: 抑制 (眼淚) / 狼吞虎嚥地喝
example: She had to gulp back her tears and continue her speech.
example_cn: 她強忍住眼淚，繼續把演講講完。
simple: He gulped down a glass of cold water.

chinese: 使...出故障 / 搞亂 / 黏住
example: The dust in the machine will eventually gum up the entire system.
example_cn: 機器裡的灰塵到最後會把整個系統卡死。
simple: Bureaucracy often gums up the legal process.

example: The suspect was gunned down by the police after he refused to surrender.
example_cn: 那名嫌犯拒絕投降，最後被警方開槍擊斃。
simple: Several people were gunned down in the street.

example: Cybercriminals tried to hack into the bank's database to steal personal information.
example_cn: 網路罪犯試圖入侵銀行的資料庫，竊取個人資訊。
simple: He was arrested for hacking into government computers.

example: The famous scientist hails from a small, remote village in the mountains.
example_cn: 那位著名的科學家來自山區一個偏遠的小村莊。
simple: The rare plant hails from the Amazon rainforest.

chinese: 磨合出 / 艱難達成 (協議)
example: The two countries finally managed to hammer out a new trade agreement.
example_cn: 那兩個國家終於成功敲定了一項新的貿易協議。
simple: They spent days hammering out the details.

example: Could you please hand around these cookies to everyone in the room?
example_cn: 可以請你把這些餅乾分給房間裡的每個人嗎？
simple: He handed around a photo of his new car.

example: You should hang onto those old records; they might be worth a lot of money someday.
example_cn: 你應該留著那些舊唱片，說不定哪天會值大錢。
simple: Hang onto your hat, it's very windy!

chinese: 團結一致 / (說法)一致、站得住腳
example: The witness's story doesn't really hang together; there are too many contradictions.
example_cn: 證人的說法前後對不上，矛盾太多了。
simple: If we don't hang together, we will fail separately.

example: Even though he had everything, he still hankered after the simple life of his childhood.
example_cn: 就算他什麼都有了，還是很懷念小時候那種簡單的生活。
simple: She hankered for a career in show business.

example: I wonder what happened to my old high school friends after graduation.
example_cn: 不知道我高中老同學畢業後都怎麼樣了。
simple: I happened to see her at the market.

chinese: 嘮叨 / 沒完沒了地說
example: My mother is always harping on about the importance of saving money.
example_cn: 我媽老是嘮叨存錢有多重要。
simple: Stop harping on about your old job!

chinese: 討論並解決 (分歧) / 達成共識
example: The board members met to hash out a final plan for the company merger.
example_cn: 董事會成員開會討論，要把公司合併的最終方案敲定下來。
simple: They spent hours hashing out their differences.

chinese: 攔截 / 阻止 / 出發
example: The police managed to head off the suspects before they could cross the border.
example_cn: 警方在嫌犯越過邊境之前成功攔住了他們。
simple: We're heading off now, see you later!

example: What time are you planning to head out to the airport tomorrow?
example_cn: 你明天打算幾點出發去機場？
simple: I'm heading out for some lunch.

example: The cut on my finger has finally started to heal over.
example_cn: 我手指上的傷口終於開始癒合了。
simple: The psychological scars may never heal up.

example: I know you're angry, but please hear me out before you make a decision.
example_cn: 我知道你很生氣，但請先聽我說完再做決定。
simple: Hear her out, she has a point.

chinese: 對抗...以降低風險 / 避險
example: Many investors buy gold as a way to hedge against inflation.
example_cn: 很多投資人買黃金來對抗通貨膨脹。
simple: Buying insurance is a hedge against disaster.

example: The warm weather and steady rain helped the crops along.
example_cn: 溫暖的天氣和穩定的降雨幫助了農作物生長。
simple: He gave me a nudge to help me along.

chinese: 包圍 / 限制 / 困住
example: The village was hemmed in by high mountains on all sides.
example_cn: 這個村莊四面被高山環繞。
simple: He felt hemmed in by the city life.

chinese: 宣布...的到來 / 預示著
example: The invention of the smartphone heralded in a new era of communication.
example_cn: 智慧型手機的發明開啟了通訊的新時代。
simple: The ringing bells heralded in the new year.

example: Don't hesitate about asking for help if you find the task too difficult.
example_cn: 如果你覺得這項任務太難，別猶豫，儘管開口求助。
simple: She hesitated over the choice of a car.

example: He keeps his most valuable stamps hidden away in a safe deposit box.
example_cn: 他把最值錢的郵票藏在保險箱裡。
simple: She hid herself away in the country.

chinese: 大幅提高 (價格) / 拉起 (衣物)
example: Stores often hike up their prices just before the holiday season begins.
example_cn: 店家常常在假期季開始前調漲價格。
simple: She hiked up her skirt to walk through the mud.

example: The heavy snow hindered the rescue team from reaching the trapped climbers.
example_cn: 大雪阻礙了救援隊抵達受困的登山客。
simple: Bad health hindered him from traveling.

example: The success of the peace talks hinges on the willingness of both sides to compromise.
example_cn: 和平談判能否成功，取決於雙方是否願意妥協。
simple: The future of the project hinges on our funding.

example: The manager hinted at possible changes in the company's structure during the meeting.
example_cn: 經理在會議上暗示公司結構可能會有所變動。
simple: What are you hinting at?

chinese: 一拍即合 / 相處得很投緣
example: The two of them hit it off immediately and have been friends ever since.
example_cn: 他們兩個一見如故，從此就成了好朋友。
pattern: A and B hit it off
simple: I didn't really hit it off with my new roommate.

chinese: 偶然發現 (主意) / 搭訕某人
example: After hours of brainstorming, we finally hit on a brilliant idea for the campaign.
example_cn: 經過好幾個小時的腦力激盪，我們終於想出行銷活動的絕妙點子。
simple: She hit on the perfect solution by accident.

example: During the war, many people tried to hoard up supplies like food and fuel.
example_cn: 戰爭期間，很多人試圖囤積糧食和燃料等物資。
simple: Squirrels hoard up nuts for the winter.

chinese: 壓制 / 限制 / 保住 (工作)
example: Despite his difficulties, he has managed to hold down a steady job for five years.
example_cn: 儘管他遭遇許多困難，還是成功保住一份穩定的工作長達五年。
simple: Try to hold down the noise.

chinese: 大發議論 / 滔滔不絕地談論
example: The professor held forth on the importance of Greek philosophy for over an hour.
example_cn: 教授滔滔不絕地講了希臘哲學的重要性，講了一個多小時。
simple: He can hold forth for hours about cars.

example: The small army managed to hold out against the invaders for several weeks.
example_cn: 這支小部隊成功抵擋入侵者好幾個星期。
simple: They held out against the enemy's pressure.

chinese: 結合在一起 / 保持團結 / (邏輯)一致
example: It was his strong leadership that held the team together during the crisis.
example_cn: 正是他強而有力的領導，才讓團隊在危機中團結一致。
simple: His argument simply doesn't hold together.

example: The outlaws were holing up in an abandoned cabin deep in the mountains.
example_cn: 那些不法之徒躲藏在深山裡一間廢棄的小木屋中。
pattern: S + hole up in + 地方
simple: They holed up for the winter.

chinese: 鎖定目標 / 集中注意力於...
example: The search engine allows users to home in on the exact information they need.
example_cn: 這個搜尋引擎能讓使用者精準找到他們需要的資訊。
simple: The missile homed in on the target.

example: Can you help me hook up the new printer to my computer?
example_cn: 你能幫我把新的印表機連到電腦上嗎？
pattern: S + hook up + N + to + N
simple: Let's hook up for coffee later.

chinese: 在萬分渺茫中抱著一線希望
example: They were hoping against hope that the missing hikers would be found alive.
example_cn: 他們抱著一線希望，期盼失蹤的登山客能活著被找到。
pattern: S + be hoping against hope that + 子句
simple: He hoped against hope for a miracle.

chinese: 闖入 / 插手 (他人事務)
example: I didn't mean to horn in on your private conversation, but I couldn't help overhearing.
example_cn: 我不是故意要插嘴你們的私人談話，只是不小心聽到了。
simple: She's always trying to horn in on my business.

example: Stop horsing around and finish your homework before your father gets home.
example_cn: 別再鬼混了，在你爸回家前把功課寫完。
simple: The kids were horsing around in the pool.

chinese: 擠在一起 / 縮成一團
example: The penguins huddled together to stay warm during the freezing Antarctic winter.
example_cn: 企鵝們擠在一起，在南極寒冷的冬天裡保暖。
simple: The frightened children huddled together in the corner.

example: As a young artist, she hungered for recognition from the critics.
example_cn: 身為一位年輕藝術家，她渴望獲得評論家的肯定。
simple: The poor villagers hunger for a better life.

chinese: 蹲下 / 潛心做事 / 準備長期應對
example: We need to hunker down and finish this project before the weekend.
example_cn: 我們得靜下心來，在週末前把這個專案搞定。
simple: The family hunkered down during the hurricane.

example: The police managed to hunt down the suspect after a long chase through the mountains.
example_cn: 警方在山區經過長途追捕後，終於逮到那名嫌犯。
simple: I finally hunted down a copy of the rare book.

example: Protesters began to hurl stones at the soldiers during the riot.
example_cn: 暴動期間，抗議者開始朝士兵丟擲石頭。
simple: They hurled abuse at the referee.

chinese: 催促前進 / 匆忙完成
example: Don't hurry through your breakfast; you have plenty of time before school.
example_cn: 早餐不用吃那麼趕，離上學還有很多時間。
simple: The teacher hurried the students along.

example: The company tried to hush up the scandal by paying the witness to remain silent.
example_cn: 公司試圖用錢讓目擊者閉嘴，想把這樁醜聞壓下來。
pattern: S + try to hush + N + up
simple: They hushed up the truth about the accident.

example: The media hyped up the new movie so much that many viewers were disappointed.
example_cn: 媒體把這部新片炒得太誇張，結果很多觀眾看了都覺得失望。
simple: The fans were all hyped up for the concert.

chinese: 識別為 / 認同為 (性別、身分)
example: The suspect was identified as a 25-year-old man from the local community.
example_cn: 嫌犯被指認是當地社區一名二十五歲的男子。
simple: She identifies as an artist.

example: During the long summer holiday, he idled away his time by playing video games.
example_cn: 漫長的暑假裡，他整天打電動消磨時間。
simple: Don't idle your life away.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: The high price of fuel has a direct impact on the cost of transportation.
example_cn: 燃料價格高漲，直接影響到運輸成本。
pattern: N + has an impact on + N
simple: Pollution impacts on the environment.

example: The butterfly was carefully impaled on a needle for the museum display.
example_cn: 那隻蝴蝶被小心地釘在針上，作為博物館的展示標本。
simple: The hunter impaled the meat on a spit.

example: His strong sense of justice impelled him to speak out against the unfair policy.
example_cn: 他強烈的正義感驅使他站出來反對這項不公平的政策。
simple: Curiosity impelled her to open the box.

example: The surgeon managed to implant a small chip in the patient's arm.
example_cn: 外科醫生成功在病人手臂裡植入了一枚小晶片。
pattern: S + implant + N + in + 地方
simple: The teacher tried to implant a love of reading in the students.

example: The new strategy will be implemented with the help of international experts.
example_cn: 這項新策略將在國際專家的協助下實施。
simple: The policy was implemented with caution.

example: Several high-ranking officials were implicated in the corruption scandal.
example_cn: 好幾位高層官員都牽扯進這起貪腐醜聞。
simple: His fingerprints implicated him in the theft.

example: The government decided to impose a new tax on luxury goods.
example_cn: 政府決定對奢侈品課徵新稅。
pattern: S + impose + N + on + 人/物
simple: I don't want to impose upon your hospitality.

example: The teacher tried to impress on the students the importance of regular review.
example_cn: 老師試圖讓學生深刻體會定期複習的重要性。
simple: He impressed upon me the need for secrecy.

example: The new model is an attempt to improve upon the existing design.
example_cn: 這款新型號是為了改良現有設計所做的嘗試。
simple: It is difficult to improve upon her performance.

example: Please make sure to include your contact information in the application form.
example_cn: 請務必在申請表上填寫你的聯絡資訊。
simple: Tax is included in the price.

chinese: 增加了 (幅度) / 在...方面增加
example: The price of vegetables has increased by twenty percent after the typhoon.
example_cn: 颱風過後，蔬菜價格漲了兩成。
simple: There has been an increase in student numbers.

example: Sometimes it's okay to indulge in a little bit of chocolate after a long day.
example_cn: 辛苦一天後，偶爾放縱自己吃點巧克力也沒關係。
simple: They indulged in their favorite hobby.

example: The patient was infected with a rare virus while traveling abroad.
example_cn: 這名病人在國外旅遊時感染了一種罕見病毒。
simple: His enthusiasm infected everyone with excitement.

example: What can we infer from the data presented in this report?
example_cn: 我們能從這份報告提供的數據推論出什麼？
pattern: S + infer + N + from + 來源
simple: He inferred from her silence that she was angry.

example: The storm inflicted severe damage on the coastal villages.
example_cn: 這場暴風雨對沿海村莊造成了嚴重破壞。
pattern: S + inflict + N (傷害/痛苦) + on + 人/物
simple: Don't inflict your problems on others.

example: Please inform us of any changes to your address as soon as possible.
example_cn: 如果你的地址有任何變更，請盡快通知我們。
simple: I have informed him about the meeting.

example: Many citizens are concerned that the new law might infringe on their privacy.
example_cn: 很多市民擔心新法可能會侵犯他們的隱私。
simple: The software infringes on existing patents.

chinese: 注入 / 充滿 (情感、特質)
example: The teacher's speech was infused with passion and inspired the whole class.
example_cn: 老師的演講充滿熱情，激勵了全班同學。
simple: The herbal tea was infused with honey.

example: She inherited her beautiful blue eyes from her grandmother.
example_cn: 她那雙漂亮的藍眼睛是遺傳自外婆的。
simple: He inherited a fortune from his uncle.

chinese: 阻礙 / 抑制 (某人做某事)
example: Fear of failure can often inhibit people from pursuing their dreams.
example_cn: 害怕失敗常常會讓人不敢去追求夢想。
simple: Cold weather inhibited the plants from growing.

chinese: 引導...入門 / 啟蒙 / 接納...加入
example: The older students initiated the freshmen into the traditions of the university.
example_cn: 學長姐帶著新生認識大學的傳統。
simple: He was initiated into the secret society.

chinese: 注射 / 注入 (金錢、活力)
example: The government plans to inject more money into the public transport system.
example_cn: 政府打算投入更多經費到公共運輸系統。
simple: The doctor injected the patient with an antibiotic.

example: The couple had their names inscribed on the inner side of their wedding rings.
example_cn: 這對夫妻把彼此的名字刻在婚戒內側。
simple: The author inscribed the book with a personal message.

example: Please insert your credit card into the slot to begin the transaction.
example_cn: 請把你的信用卡插入插槽來開始交易。
simple: He inserted a key into the lock.

chinese: 混進 / 鑽營 / 影射
example: He managed to insinuate himself into the group and gain their trust.
example_cn: 他設法悄悄融入那個團體，並取得了他們的信任。
simple: She insinuated her ideas into the conversation.

example: My grandmother always insists on everyone finishing their entire meal.
example_cn: 我奶奶總是堅持每個人都要把飯吃完。
simple: He insisted on paying for dinner.

example: Safety officers inspect the building for any potential fire hazards every year.
example_cn: 安全檢查人員每年都會檢查建築物有沒有潛在的火災危險。
simple: Customs officers inspected the luggage for drugs.

example: The general's bravery inspired confidence in his soldiers.
example_cn: 將軍的勇敢讓士兵們充滿信心。
pattern: N + inspire + 情感 + in + 人
simple: The leader inspired the team with his vision.

example: We need to install a new security system in the house before we move in.
example_cn: 我們搬進去之前，需要在房子裡安裝新的保全系統。
pattern: S + install + N + in + 地方
simple: The software was installed in the main server.

example: It is important to instil a sense of responsibility in children from an early age.
example_cn: 從小培養孩子的責任感是很重要的。
simple: Her parents instilled in her a love of music.

chinese: 灌輸 (想法、價值觀) 給...
example: It is important to instil a sense of confidence into children from an early age.
example_cn: 從小培養孩子的自信心是很重要的。
simple: Good teachers instil a love of learning into their students.

example: The trainer will instruct you in the proper use of the gym equipment.
example_cn: 教練會指導你如何正確使用健身器材。
simple: He instructs students in the art of debate.

chinese: 使絕緣 / 隔絕 / 免受 (負面影響)
example: The building is well insulated against noise from the busy street outside.
example_cn: 這棟建築的隔音效果很好，不受外面繁忙街道的噪音干擾。
simple: Parents try to insulate their children from the harsh realities of life.

example: Homeowners are strongly advised to insure their property against fire and theft.
example_cn: 強烈建議屋主為房屋投保火災和竊盜險。
simple: The company insured itself against financial loss.

example: It can be difficult for immigrants to integrate into a completely new culture.
example_cn: 對移民來說，要融入一個全新的文化並不容易。
simple: The new system integrates well with our existing software.

chinese: 打算給 / 預定用途為...
example: The book was originally intended for children, but it became popular among adults too.
example_cn: 這本書原本是寫給小孩看的，但後來在大人之間也變得很受歡迎。
pattern: S + be intended for + 人/用途
simple: This area is intended for authorized personnel only.

example: Social media platforms allow people to interact with others from all around the world.
example_cn: 社群媒體平台讓人們可以跟世界各地的人互動。
simple: How does this chemical interact with water?

example: You should not interfere in other people's personal matters unless they ask for help.
example_cn: 除非別人主動求助，不然你不應該插手他們的私事。
simple: The noise interfered with my sleep.

example: Her silence was interpreted as agreement by the rest of the committee.
example_cn: 她的沉默被委員會其他人解讀為同意。
simple: We interpreted his comments as a threat.

example: The witness claimed that he was intimidated into giving a false testimony.
example_cn: 證人聲稱他是被威脅才做出虛假證詞的。
simple: Don't let them intimidate you into quitting.

example: I am sorry if I am intruding on your private conversation.
example_cn: 如果打擾到你們私下談話，我很抱歉。
simple: The construction noise intruded on our peace.

chinese: 使應接不暇 / (大量) 淹沒
example: The customer service center was inundated with calls after the system failure.
example_cn: 系統故障後，客服中心被電話淹沒了。
simple: We were inundated with requests for tickets.

example: Most students are involved in at least one extracurricular activity.
example_cn: 大部分學生至少會參加一項課外活動。
simple: The police are looking for anyone involved in the crime.

example: The patient had to be isolated from others to prevent the spread of the virus.
example_cn: 那位病人必須與其他人隔離，以防止病毒擴散。
simple: The remote island was isolated from the mainland for centuries.

example: A strange smoke began to issue from the cracks in the ground.
example_cn: 一股奇怪的煙霧開始從地面的裂縫中冒出來。
simple: All our problems issue from a lack of communication.

chinese: 大幅提高 (價格) / 用千斤頂舉起
example: Oil companies have jacked up the price of gasoline again this week.
example_cn: 石油公司這週又把汽油價格調漲了。
simple: We need to jack up the car to change the tire.

example: The driver had to jam on the brakes to avoid hitting the cat.
example_cn: 司機緊急踩煞車，以免撞到那隻貓。
simple: He jammed on the brakes when the light turned red.

chinese: 使活潑 / 使更有趣 / 裝飾
example: You can jazz up a simple room with some colorful paintings and plants.
example_cn: 你可以用一些色彩鮮豔的畫作和植物，讓簡單的房間變得活潑起來。
simple: Let's jazz up the presentation with some music.

example: The crowd began to jeer at the players after they lost the game.
example_cn: 球隊輸球後，群眾開始對球員發出噓聲。
simple: It is unkind to jeer at other people's failures.

example: They are planning to jet off to Japan for their honeymoon next week.
example_cn: 他們計畫下週搭飛機去日本度蜜月。
pattern: S + jet off to + 地方
simple: The celebrities jetted off to a private island.

example: Everyone was singing, and she decided to join in for the last chorus.
example_cn: 大家都在唱歌，她決定在最後一段副歌時加入。
simple: Would you like to join in the game?

example: It is important to know when it is appropriate to joke about a serious matter.
example_cn: 知道什麼時候適合拿嚴肅的事情開玩笑，這點很重要。
simple: They were joking about their old school days.

chinese: 草草記下 / 匆匆寫下
example: I jotted down his phone number on a small piece of paper.
example_cn: 我把他的電話號碼隨手記在一張小紙片上。
simple: Let me jot down a few ideas before I forget.

example: Judging by the look on his face, he hasn't received very good news.
example_cn: 從他臉上的表情來看，他應該沒收到什麼好消息。
simple: Don't judge a book by its cover.

chinese: 耍弄 (球等) / 盡力同時應付 (多項事務)
example: As a busy mother, she has to juggle with work and childcare every day.
example_cn: 身為一個忙碌的媽媽，她每天都得同時應付工作和帶小孩。
pattern: S + juggle with + A and B
simple: He is good at juggling with many tasks at once.

example: She jumped at the chance to study abroad when the scholarship was offered.
example_cn: 獎學金一提供，她就立刻把握機會出國留學。
simple: I would jump at the offer if I were you.

chinese: 跳進去 / 突然插嘴 / 急於加入
example: The water was freezing, but the brave boy jumped in without hesitation.
example_cn: 水超冰的，但那個勇敢的男生二話不說就跳進去了。
simple: Feel free to jump in if you have any questions.

example: You shouldn't jump to conclusions before you have all the facts.
example_cn: 在掌握所有事實之前，你不該隨便下結論。
simple: Everyone jumped to the same conclusion.

example: The sharp rocks jut out from the side of the mountain, making it dangerous for climbers.
example_cn: 尖銳的岩石從山壁突出來，讓攀岩者很危險。
simple: The balcony juts out over the garden.

example: He was so exhausted from the heat that he almost keeled over in the middle of the street.
example_cn: 他熱到快虛脫，差點在馬路中間昏倒。
simple: The small boat keeled over in the strong wind.

example: The two rival fans had to be kept apart by a line of police officers.
example_cn: 那兩個敵對的粉絲必須靠一排警察隔開。
simple: It is hard to keep the two kids apart when they are fighting.

example: Learning a language is difficult, but if you keep at it, you will eventually succeed.
example_cn: 學語言很難，但只要持續努力，你終究會成功。
simple: She kept at her work until it was finished.

chinese: 阻擋 / 隱瞞 (秘密) / 阻止前進
example: The police used barriers to keep the excited crowd back from the stage.
example_cn: 警方用護欄把興奮的群眾擋在舞台外面。
simple: I felt he was keeping something back from me.

example: The teacher kept the student behind after class to discuss his poor test results.
example_cn: 老師課後把學生留下來，討論他考試成績不理想的事。
simple: They were kept behind to finish the task.

chinese: 壓制 / 控制 (音量、成本) / 忍住不吐
example: Please try to keep your voice down; the baby is sleeping in the next room.
example_cn: 請盡量小聲一點；寶寶正在隔壁房間睡覺。
simple: The government is trying to keep inflation down.

chinese: 繼續前進 / 堅持下去
example: Even though you are tired, you must keep going if you want to finish the race.
example_cn: 就算累了，想跑完全程還是得繼續撐下去。
simple: The engine kept going all night.

example: It is a good idea to keep in with your neighbors in case you ever need their help.
example_cn: 跟鄰居保持良好關係是個好主意，萬一哪天需要他們幫忙。
simple: He tried to keep in with the boss.

example: I try to keep out of my colleagues' arguments as much as possible.
example_cn: 我盡量不摻和同事之間的爭執。
simple: Keep out of the kitchen while I'm cooking.

example: It's difficult for traditional companies to keep up with the rapid pace of digital change.
example_cn: 傳統公司很難跟上數位變革的快速腳步。
pattern: S + (struggle to) keep up with + N
simple: She reads the news to keep up with current events.

example: You need to key in your four-digit PIN to access your bank account.
example_cn: 你需要輸入四位數的密碼才能登入銀行帳戶。
simple: Key in the data carefully.

chinese: 討論 (主意) / 閒逛 / 隨處亂放
example: We kicked a few ideas around for the marketing campaign during the meeting.
example_cn: 會議上我們針對行銷活動隨便丟了一些點子出來討論。
simple: There's an old pair of shoes kicking around in the garage.

chinese: 放鬆 / 休息 / (非法)回扣
example: After a long week of hard work, I just want to kick back and watch a movie.
example_cn: 辛苦工作一整週後，我只想放鬆下來看部電影。
simple: We kicked back on the beach for the whole afternoon.

chinese: 開始起作用 / 貢獻 (金錢)
example: The medicine usually takes about thirty minutes to kick in and relieve the pain.
example_cn: 這個藥通常大概三十分鐘後才會開始發揮作用、緩解疼痛。
simple: The central heating kicks in automatically.

example: The noisy students were kicked out of the library by the staff.
example_cn: 那些吵鬧的學生被圖書館工作人員趕了出去。
pattern: S + be kicked out of + N
simple: He was kicked out of the club for fighting.

example: The strong winds kicked up a huge cloud of dust in the desert.
example_cn: 強風在沙漠中颳起了一大片塵雲。
simple: The engine is kicking up a lot of smoke.

example: Don't take him seriously; he's just kidding around with you.
example_cn: 別當真啦，他只是跟你鬧著玩的。
simple: Stop kidding around and get to work.

example: The usage of pesticides eventually killed off many of the beneficial insects.
example_cn: 農藥的使用最後把很多益蟲都殺光了。
simple: The writer decided to kill off the main character.

example: The hikers were kitted out with the latest waterproof gear before their trip.
example_cn: 那些登山客出發前都配備了最新的防水裝備。
pattern: S + be kitted out with + N
simple: The laboratory is kitted out with expensive equipment.

example: He had to kneel down on the floor to look for his lost ring under the sofa.
example_cn: 他得跪在地板上找掉在沙發底下的戒指。
simple: She knelt down to tie her shoelaces.

example: The disaster served to knit the whole community together in support of the victims.
example_cn: 這場災難反而讓整個社區團結起來幫助受災戶。
simple: Broken bones usually take weeks to knit together.

chinese: 下班 / 停止工作 / 扣除 (金額)
example: What time do you usually knock off on Friday afternoons?
example_cn: 你們星期五下午通常幾點下班？
simple: They knocked ten dollars off the price.

example: The cat accidentally knocked over the vase while chasing a butterfly.
example_cn: 那隻貓在追蝴蝶的時候不小心把花瓶撞倒了。
simple: A truck knocked over a power pole.

example: The city of Tainan is known as the cultural capital of Taiwan.
example_cn: 台南市被稱為台灣的文化首都。
simple: You can know a person by the friends he keeps.

chinese: 貼上標籤為 / 把...歸類為
example: The product was clearly labeled as 'organic' to attract health-conscious consumers.
example_cn: 產品上清楚標示「有機」，來吸引注重健康的消費者。
simple: Critics labeled the movie as a masterpiece.

chinese: 苦心鑽研 / 在...上煞費苦心
example: The artist labored over the painting for months to get every detail perfect.
example_cn: 那位藝術家為了把每個細節都畫到位，花了幾個月時間苦心創作。
simple: She labored over the final chapter of her book.

example: The runner sat on the bench to lace up his sneakers before the race began.
example_cn: 跑者在比賽開始前坐在長椅上綁好他的運動鞋。
simple: He laced up his boots and headed out.

example: Our company's technology is starting to lag behind its competitors in the global market.
example_cn: 我們公司的技術在全球市場上開始落後競爭對手了。
simple: The country lags behind in digital education.

chinese: 為...哀悼 / 為...感到遺憾
example: Environmentalists lament over the loss of the pristine forest area.
example_cn: 環保人士對原始森林區的消失感到惋惜。
simple: They lamented over the missed opportunity.

chinese: 陷入 (困境) / 最終落得
example: His reckless behavior eventually caused him to land in prison.
example_cn: 他魯莽的行為最後讓他進了監獄。
simple: If you keep spending like that, you'll land up in debt.

chinese: 欣然接受 / 舔食 / (比喻)極度喜愛
example: The audience lapped up every word of the singer's inspiring speech.
example_cn: 觀眾對那位歌手激勵人心的演講聽得津津有味。
simple: The puppy lapped up the milk.

chinese: 陷入 (某種狀態) / 墮入
example: After the long argument, the room lapsed into a long, uncomfortable silence.
example_cn: 經過長久的爭論後，房間裡陷入一陣漫長又尷尬的沉默。
simple: He lapsed into unconsciousness.

chinese: 猛烈抨擊 / 猛力擊打
example: The stressed manager lashed out at his staff during the meeting this morning.
example_cn: 壓力很大的主管今天早上在會議上對員工大發脾氣。
simple: The horse lashed out with its back legs.

chinese: 附著於 / 緊抓不放 / 理解
example: The media latched onto the scandal and wouldn't stop reporting about it.
example_cn: 媒體緊咬著那樁醜聞不放，一直報導個沒完。
simple: The baby latched onto its mother's hand.

chinese: 貿然開始 / 興致勃勃地投入
example: As soon as he walked in, he launched into a long story about his vacation.
example_cn: 他一走進來就開始滔滔不絕地講他度假的故事。
simple: She launched into a fierce argument.

chinese: 擱置 / 撇開 / 儲蓄
example: They decided to lay aside their differences and work together for the good of the project.
example_cn: 他們決定放下歧見，為了專案順利而合作。
simple: He laid aside his book when I entered.

example: His father really laid into him after he found out he had skipped school.
example_cn: 他爸爸發現他翹課之後，狠狠教訓了他一頓。
simple: Critics laid into the new movie.

chinese: 解雇 (裁員) / 停止 (騷擾、食用)
example: The company had to lay off hundreds of workers due to the economic downturn.
example_cn: 公司因為經濟不景氣，不得不裁掉幾百名員工。
simple: Lay off me! I'm trying my best.

example: The events that led up to the war are still being debated by historians today.
example_cn: 導致戰爭爆發的種種事件，至今歷史學家仍在爭論不休。
simple: There were several clues leading up to the discovery.

example: I spent the afternoon leafing through some old fashion magazines in the waiting room.
example_cn: 我整個下午都在候診室裡翻閱一些舊的時尚雜誌。
simple: He leafed through the book quickly.

example: The news of the secret merger leaked out before it was officially announced.
example_cn: 秘密合併的消息在正式公布之前就走漏了。
simple: Details of the contract leaked out to the press.

chinese: 依靠 / 依賴 / 向...施壓
example: You can always lean on your friends for emotional support during difficult times.
example_cn: 在困難的時候，你隨時可以依靠朋友獲得情感上的支持。
pattern: S + lean on + 人 + for + N
simple: The gang leaned on the shopkeeper to pay them.

example: He leaped at the chance to study abroad when the scholarship was offered.
example_cn: 獎學金一提出來，他馬上抓住機會出國留學。
simple: She leaped at the offer of a new job.

example: It is important to learn from your mistakes so that you don't repeat them in the future.
example_cn: 從錯誤中學習很重要，這樣以後才不會再犯。
simple: We can learn a lot from the history of other countries.

chinese: 不予理會 / 擱置 (暫不討論)
example: Let's leave aside the cost for a moment and focus on the benefits of the project.
example_cn: 我們先暫時不談成本，專注在這個計畫的好處上。
simple: Leaving aside his lack of experience, he is a good candidate.

chinese: 不關 (燈、電器) / 留在上面
example: Please don't leave the lights on when you leave the classroom.
example_cn: 離開教室的時候，請不要把燈開著。
simple: He left the engine on while he ran into the shop.

example: The library doesn't lend out rare manuscripts because they are too valuable.
example_cn: 圖書館不外借珍貴的手稿，因為它們太有價值了。
simple: I don't like lending out my tools.

example: He can't even boil an egg, let alone cook a complex three-course meal.
example_cn: 他連煮個蛋都不會，更別說做一頓三道菜的複雜料理了。
pattern: S + can't V1, let alone V2
simple: She doesn't have a bicycle, let alone a car.

chinese: 放手 / 釋放 / 解雇
example: You need to let go of the past and focus on your future goals.
example_cn: 你需要放下過去，專注在未來的目標上。
simple: The company let go of twenty employees.

example: We decided to let her in on our secret plan for the surprise party.
example_cn: 我們決定讓她知道我們為驚喜派對準備的秘密計畫。
pattern: S + let + 人 + in on + N
simple: He let me in on his little secret.

chinese: 停止 / 減弱 / 鬆勁
example: The heavy rain didn't let up all day, so we had to stay indoors.
example_cn: 大雨一整天都沒有停，所以我們只好待在室內。
simple: If the work doesn't let up soon, I'll be exhausted.

chinese: 對 (某人) 坦白說實話
example: I'll level with you: your chances of getting the job are very low.
example_cn: 我跟你坦白說：你得到這份工作的機會非常低。
simple: She finally leveled with me about her problems.

chinese: 在前面 (指未來的困難或路途)
example: We don't know what challenges lie ahead of us in the new year.
example_cn: 我們不知道新的一年前方會有什麼挑戰等著我們。
simple: A long and difficult journey lies ahead.

chinese: 是...的原因 / 隱藏在...背後
example: What really lies behind his sudden decision to quit his job?
example_cn: 他突然決定辭職，背後真正的原因到底是什麼？
simple: Curiosity lies behind most scientific discoveries.

example: The criminal decided to lie low for a few weeks until the police stopped searching.
example_cn: 那名罪犯決定躲藏幾個星期，等警方停止搜查再說。
simple: He's lying low after the scandal.

chinese: (火箭、飛機) 起飛 / 發射
example: The space shuttle lifted off exactly on schedule this morning.
example_cn: 太空梭今天早上準時升空。
simple: We watched the rocket lift off.

example: You're too serious about everything; you need to lighten up a bit!
example_cn: 你對什麼事都太認真了，需要放輕鬆一點！
simple: Lighten up! It was just a joke.

example: The manager decided to limit the meeting to thirty minutes to save time.
example_cn: 經理決定把會議限制在三十分鐘以內，這樣比較省時間。
simple: Please limit your speech to five minutes.

chinese: (感覺、氣味) 持續很久 / 徘徊不前
example: The scent of her perfume lingered on in the room long after she had left.
example_cn: 她離開很久之後，房間裡還留著她身上的香水味。
simple: His gaze lingered on the old photo.

chinese: 連結 / 會合 / 與...合作
example: The two local charities decided to link up to provide better service for the homeless.
example_cn: 兩家當地慈善機構決定聯手合作，為遊民提供更好的服務。
simple: The computer systems were linked up last week.

example: The mother sat in the dark, listening for the sound of her baby waking up.
example_cn: 媽媽坐在黑暗中，仔細聽著寶寶醒來的聲音。
simple: I'm listening for the doorbell.

example: I suspect that someone was listening in on our private phone conversation.
example_cn: 我懷疑有人偷聽我們的私人電話交談。
simple: Don't listen in on other people's secrets.

chinese: 布滿 / 到處都是 (通常指亂七八糟的東西)
example: The park was littered with empty bottles and trash after the music festival.
example_cn: 音樂節結束後，公園裡到處都是空瓶子和垃圾。
simple: Her essay was littered with spelling mistakes.

chinese: 遵循 (原則、規則) 生活 / 靠...維生
example: He is a man of integrity who always lives by his professional principles.
example_cn: 他是個正直的人，始終堅守自己的職業原則。
simple: They live by the sea.

chinese: 使人忘記 (過去的尷尬或錯誤)
example: If you make such a silly mistake now, you'll never live it down.
example_cn: 如果你現在犯這種愚蠢的錯誤，以後一輩子都會被人笑。
pattern: S + will never live + it + down
simple: He's still trying to live down his failure.

example: Some students still live off their parents even after they have finished university.
example_cn: 有些學生大學畢業了還在靠父母養。
simple: Parasites live off other animals.

chinese: 經歷過 (災難、困境) 並倖存
example: The elderly man had lived through two world wars and many economic crises.
example_cn: 那位老先生經歷過兩次世界大戰和多次經濟危機。
simple: I don't know how they lived through that storm.

chinese: 符合 / 達到 (期望、標準)
example: The new movie didn't quite live up to the high expectations of the fans.
example_cn: 這部新電影沒有達到粉絲們的高度期待。
simple: He struggled to live up to his father's reputation.

example: They loaded up the truck with camping gear and headed for the mountains.
example_cn: 他們把露營裝備裝上卡車，然後往山區出發。
pattern: S + load up + N + with + N
simple: The bus was loaded up with tourists.

example: Stop loafing around and do something useful with your time!
example_cn: 別再混日子了，做點有意義的事吧！
simple: He spent the afternoon loafing around the house.

chinese: 遊說支持 / 遊說反對
example: Environmental groups are lobbying for stricter laws against plastic pollution.
example_cn: 環保團體正在遊說政府制定更嚴格的塑膠污染法規。
simple: They are lobbying against the new tax.

example: She keeps her most valuable jewelry locked away in a safe deposit box.
example_cn: 她把最貴重的珠寶鎖在銀行的保險箱裡。
simple: The violent criminal was locked away for life.

example: You need to log in to your account before you can make any changes.
example_cn: 你需要先登入帳號，才能進行任何修改。
simple: Please log off before leaving the computer.

chinese: 閒逛 / 遊蕩 (常用於指逗留在某處無所事事)
example: The police asked the group of teenagers not to loiter around the store entrance.
example_cn: 警方要求那群青少年不要在商店門口逗留。
simple: No loitering allowed!

chinese: 展望未來 / 預作打算
example: We need to look ahead and prepare for the challenges of the next decade.
example_cn: 我們需要放眼未來，為接下來十年的挑戰做好準備。
simple: Look ahead to the future.

chinese: 環視 / 參觀 / 尋找
example: We spent the whole morning looking around the ancient ruins of the castle.
example_cn: 我們花了一整個上午在古城堡遺跡裡四處參觀。
simple: I'll look around and see if I can find my keys.

example: He had to look away during the scary scene in the horror movie.
example_cn: 恐怖片演到嚇人的橋段時，他不得不把視線移開。
simple: Don't look away when I'm talking to you.

example: You shouldn't look down on people just because they have less money than you.
example_cn: 你不應該因為別人錢比你少就看不起他們。
simple: She looks down on anyone who hasn't been to university.

example: Could you please look in on my grandmother while I am away on a business trip?
example_cn: 我出差的時候，可以麻煩你順便去看看我奶奶嗎？
simple: I'll look in on you tomorrow to see how you are.

chinese: 留意 / 當心 / 照顧
example: You should look out for pickpockets when you are in crowded tourist areas.
example_cn: 在人多擁擠的觀光區，你要小心扒手。
simple: Friends should look out for each other.

chinese: (陰影、問題) 隱約出現 / 逼近
example: A large dark shape suddenly loomed up out of the heavy fog.
example_cn: 一個巨大的黑影突然從濃霧中浮現出來。
simple: The exam deadline is looming ahead.

chinese: 放鬆 / (肌肉) 活動開來
example: The athlete did some stretches to help his muscles loosen up before the race.
example_cn: 那位運動員在比賽前做了一些伸展動作，幫助肌肉放鬆。
simple: Drink a little wine to loosen up.

chinese: 對...稱王稱霸 / 頤指氣使
example: Ever since he was promoted, he has been trying to lord it over his former colleagues.
example_cn: 自從他升官之後，就一直想在以前的同事面前擺架子。
simple: Don't try to lord it over me!

example: Many traditional small shops are losing out to large online retailers.
example_cn: 很多傳統小店都輸給了大型網路零售商。
simple: We lost out to a better offer.

chinese: 使放鬆警惕而... / 哄騙
example: The calm weather lulled the sailors into a false sense of security.
example_cn: 平靜的天氣讓船員們鬆懈了警覺心。
simple: The music lulled the baby into a deep sleep.

chinese: 混為一談 / 歸併在一起
example: You cannot just lump all these different problems together; each one needs a specific solution.
example_cn: 你不能把這些不同的問題全部混為一談，每個都需要特定的解決方法。
pattern: S + lump + A and B + together
simple: Children of different ages were lumped together in one class.

example: The rival company tried to lure away our best engineers with higher salaries.
example_cn: 競爭對手公司試圖用更高的薪水挖走我們最優秀的工程師。
pattern: S + lure + 人 + away (from + 地方)
simple: The scent of food lured the dog away.

example: She spent the whole afternoon luxuriating in a warm bubble bath.
example_cn: 她整個下午都泡在溫暖的泡泡浴裡享受。
simple: They luxuriated in the sunshine.

example: He made as if to speak, but then changed his mind and remained silent.
example_cn: 他好像要開口說話，但後來改變主意，保持沉默。
pattern: S + make as if + to V
simple: She made as if she hadn't heard me.

chinese: 攜帶...逃走 / 偷走 / 幹掉
example: The thieves made away with thousands of dollars worth of cash and jewelry.
example_cn: 那些小偷帶著價值好幾千美元的現金和珠寶逃走了。
simple: The fox made away with a chicken.

example: The children like to make believe that the cardboard box is a spaceship.
example_cn: 孩子們喜歡假裝那個紙箱是一艘太空船。
simple: It's all just make-believe.

chinese: 成功 / 趕上 / 活下來
example: She worked very hard for years and finally made it as a professional singer.
example_cn: 她努力了好幾年，終於如願成為一名專業歌手。
simple: I'm sorry, I can't make it to your party tonight.

example: The robber made off with her purse while she was distracted by the street performer.
example_cn: 當她被街頭藝人分散注意力時，搶匪趁機拿走了她的錢包。
simple: Someone made off with my bicycle.

chinese: 改造 / 翻新 / 轉讓 (財產)
example: The stylists decided to make over her entire appearance for the television show.
example_cn: 造型師決定為了那個電視節目徹底改造她的外型。
simple: He made over his property to his son.

example: He’s always trying to make up to the boss in the hope of getting a promotion.
example_cn: 他總是試著討好老闆，希望能獲得升遷。
simple: Stop trying to make up to me!

chinese: 像個男子漢 (勇敢面對) / 振作
example: You need to man up and face the consequences of your actions.
example_cn: 你要拿出男子氣概，面對自己行為的後果。
simple: Man up and tell her the truth.

example: This high-quality paper is manufactured from recycled materials.
example_cn: 這種高品質的紙張是用回收材料製造的。
pattern: S + be manufactured from + 來源
simple: Plastics are manufactured from oil.

example: He spent the morning nailing up boards to protect the windows from the storm.
example_cn: 他花了一整個早上把木板釘起來，保護窗戶不受暴風雨侵襲。
simple: Nail up the notice on the wall.

example: The street was named after a famous poet who lived in this neighborhood.
example_cn: 那條街是以一位住在這個社區的著名詩人命名的。
pattern: S + be named after + 人/物
simple: He was named after his grandfather.

example: The committee has narrowed the list of candidates down to just three people.
example_cn: 委員會已經把候選人名單縮小到只剩三個人。
pattern: S + narrow + N + down to + 數量
simple: We need to narrow down the problem to its core.

chinese: 導航穿過 / 設法應對 (複雜情況)
example: The captain skillfully navigated the ship through the narrow channel.
example_cn: 船長熟練地駕駛船隻通過那條狹窄的水道。
simple: It's hard to navigate through the city's complex bureaucracy.

example: The management is currently negotiating with the union for better working conditions.
example_cn: 管理階層目前正在跟工會協商，爭取更好的工作條件。
simple: They refuse to negotiate with terrorists.

chinese: 安臥 / 依偎 / 坐落於
example: The small cottage nestles in a quiet valley surrounded by high mountains.
example_cn: 那間小木屋坐落在一個被高山環繞的寧靜山谷裡。
simple: The baby nestled in her mother's arms.

chinese: 與...建立聯繫 / 建立人脈
example: The conference provides a great opportunity to network with professionals in the field.
example_cn: 這場會議提供了絕佳的機會，可以跟這個領域的專業人士交流人脈。
simple: You should network with other students at the event.

chinese: 一點一點地咬 / 逐漸消耗
example: Rising prices are slowly nibbling away at our monthly savings.
example_cn: 物價上漲正慢慢侵蝕我們每個月的儲蓄。
simple: The mouse was nibbling at a piece of cheese.

example: I was so tired during the lecture that I accidentally nodded off for a few minutes.
example_cn: 上課時我太累了，不小心打瞌睡了幾分鐘。
simple: He nodded off while watching the movie.

example: The actress was nominated for an Academy Award for her outstanding performance.
example_cn: 那位女演員因為出色的表現，獲得了奧斯卡獎提名。
simple: They nominated him for the position of chairman.

chinese: 到處打聽 / 窺探 (非正式)
example: The reporter was caught nosing around the private property without permission.
example_cn: 記者未經許可就在私人土地上到處打探，當場被抓到。
simple: I caught him nosing around in my desk.

example: Please note down the instructions so you won't forget them later.
example_cn: 請把指示記下來，這樣你之後才不會忘記。
simple: She noted down his phone number.

example: You will be notified of the test results by email next week.
example_cn: 下週你會透過電子郵件收到考試結果的通知。
simple: Please notify the manager of any changes.

chinese: 輕推以促使 / 緩慢引導進入
example: The coach managed to nudge his team into a more positive mindset.
example_cn: 教練成功引導他的隊伍轉向更積極的心態。
simple: They nudged her into accepting the job.

chinese: 名列...之中 / 被歸類為
example: This rare species numbers among the most endangered animals in the world.
example_cn: 這個稀有物種名列世界上瀕危程度最高的動物之一。
simple: I am proud to number you among my friends.

chinese: 悉心照料 (某人) 度過 (病期、難關)
example: She stayed at home for a week to nurse her sick brother through the flu.
example_cn: 她在家待了一整個星期，照顧她生病的哥哥度過流感。
pattern: S + nurse + 人 + through + N (疾病/難關)
simple: The nurse helped him through the recovery.

chinese: 商量解決 / 費力想出
example: We need to sit down and nut out a solution to this problem before the deadline.
example_cn: 我們需要在截止日前坐下來好好想辦法解決這個問題。
simple: They managed to nut out a deal.

chinese: 應邀提供 / 施以恩惠
example: The pianist was happy to oblige with an encore after the enthusiastic applause.
example_cn: 鋼琴家在熱烈的掌聲後，很樂意加演一曲。
simple: Would you oblige me with a match?

example: You shouldn't obsess over small mistakes; focus on the big picture instead.
example_cn: 你不該一直糾結在小錯誤上，要專注看整體大局。
simple: She is obsessing over her weight.

example: You can obtain the necessary application forms from the front desk.
example_cn: 你可以從櫃檯拿到所需的申請表格。
pattern: S + obtain + N + from + 來源
simple: Information can be obtained from the internet.

example: He occupied himself with reading a book while waiting for the train.
example_cn: 他在等火車的時候看書打發時間。
simple: She is occupied with her research.

example: The illegal building was found to offend against several safety regulations.
example_cn: 那棟違建被發現違反了好幾項安全規定。
simple: His actions offended against all laws of decency.

example: The company's losses in the first quarter were offset by strong sales in the second.
example_cn: 公司第一季的虧損被第二季的強勁銷售給補了回來。
simple: The price increase will be offset by better service.

example: The large windows in the living room open onto a beautiful garden.
example_cn: 客廳的大窗戶一打開就是一座漂亮的花園。
pattern: N (門/窗) + open(s) onto + 地方
simple: The kitchen opens into the dining area.

example: The surgeon had to operate on the patient immediately to save his life.
example_cn: 外科醫生必須立刻幫病人動手術才能救他的命。
simple: The doctor operated on my knee.

example: I owe my success to the continuous support and encouragement of my parents.
example_cn: 我的成功要歸功於父母一直以來的支持與鼓勵。
pattern: S + owe + N + to + 人/物
simple: He owes ten dollars to me.

chinese: 承認 (錯誤或罪行) / 招認
example: The teacher asked who broke the window, but nobody was brave enough to own up.
example_cn: 老師問是誰打破窗戶的，但沒有人有勇氣承認。
simple: He finally owned up to the mistake.

chinese: 浮現在腦海 / 被想到
example: It suddenly occurred to me that I had left my house keys in the office.
example_cn: 我突然想到我把家裡鑰匙留在辦公室了。
pattern: It + occur(s) to + 人 + that + 子句
simple: Did it ever occur to you that he might be lying?

example: After much consideration, they eventually opted for the smaller, more affordable car.
example_cn: 考慮了很久之後，他們最後選擇了那台比較小、也比較便宜的車。
simple: I opted for a quiet night at home.

example: Nobody likes working with him because he’s always trying to order people around.
example_cn: 沒有人喜歡跟他一起工作，因為他老是想指揮別人。
simple: Stop ordering me around!

chinese: 用...取消 / 抵銷 / 否定
example: The benefit of the exercise was negated with the heavy meal that followed.
example_cn: 運動的好處被接下來那頓大餐給抵消掉了。
simple: The positive results were negated by a single mistake.

example: The two old friends spent the afternoon nattering about their school days.
example_cn: 那兩位老朋友整個下午都在聊他們學生時代的事。
simple: She can natter on for hours.

example: The witness began to note down the license plate number of the car.
example_cn: 目擊者開始記下那輛車的車牌號碼。
simple: He noted down the appointment time.

chinese: 提供 / 貢獻 / 祈禱
example: The charity asked people to offer up any old clothes they no longer needed.
example_cn: 慈善機構請大家捐出不再需要的舊衣服。
simple: They offered up prayers for the victims.

example: The weather was terrible; as a result, the outdoor concert was canceled.
example_cn: 天氣糟透了，結果戶外音樂會就被取消了。
pattern: S + V; as a result, S + V
simple: He worked very hard and, as a result, he was promoted.

example: The species went extinct as a result of loss of natural habitat.
example_cn: 這個物種因為失去自然棲息地而滅絕了。
pattern: S + V + as a result of + N
simple: He died as a result of his injuries.

example: The heavy rain resulted in several traffic accidents across the city.
example_cn: 大雨導致全市發生了好幾起交通事故。
pattern: N (原因) + result(s) in + N (結果)
simple: The experiment resulted in a huge success.

example: His failure resulted from a lack of preparation and effort.
example_cn: 他的失敗是因為準備不足又不夠努力。
pattern: N (結果) + result(s) from + N (原因)
simple: The fire resulted from a short circuit.

chinese: 直接結果 / 間接結果
example: The success of the project was a direct result of everyone's hard work.
example_cn: 這個計畫的成功是大家努力的直接成果。
pattern: N + is a direct result of + N
simple: Pollution is an indirect result of industrial growth.

chinese: 必然結果 / 邏輯上的結果
example: If you don't study, failing the exam is the inevitable result.
example_cn: 如果你不讀書，考試不及格是必然的結果。
pattern: N + be the inevitable result of + N
simple: Poverty is often the logical result of long-term unemployment.

type: 名詞 + 名詞 / 形容詞 + 名詞
example: The end result was exactly what we had hoped for at the beginning.
example_cn: 最終的結果，正好就是我們一開始所期望的。
pattern: The end result of + N + be + N
simple: We are still waiting for the final results of the election.

example: The clean-up operation achieved a satisfactory result in restoring the beach.
example_cn: 這次的清理行動，在恢復沙灘原貌方面達到了令人滿意的成果。
simple: The negotiations did not yield a satisfactory result.

chinese: 令人失望的結果 / 意料之外的結果
example: The team was upset by the disappointing result of the final match.
example_cn: 團隊因為決賽的成績不理想而感到很難過。
pattern: N + be a disappointing result for + 人
simple: The test produced an unexpected result.

chinese: 具體的成果 / 有形的結果
example: The new policy has produced tangible results in reducing poverty within a year.
example_cn: 這項新政策在一年內就對減少貧窮產生了具體的成效。
simple: We are looking for tangible results, not just promises.

phrase: get a result / obtain a result
chinese: 得到結果 / 獲得結果
example: You will have to wait for a week to obtain the results of your medical check-up.
example_cn: 你得等一個星期才能拿到健檢報告。
pattern: S + wait to obtain the results
simple: The scientist worked hard to get a result.

phrase: announce the results / publish the results
chinese: 宣布結果 / 公佈結果
example: The election commission will announce the results at midnight.
example_cn: 選舉委員會會在午夜公布結果。
pattern: S + will announce the results of + N
simple: The university published the results on its website.

example: The company cut costs drastically, with the result that many employees lost their jobs.
example_cn: 公司大幅削減成本，結果導致許多員工失業。
pattern: S + V, with the result that + S + V
simple: He missed the train, with the result that he was late for the interview.

chinese: 徒勞無功地 / 沒有結果
example: The police searched the forest for three days without result.
example_cn: 警方在森林裡搜尋了三天，一無所獲。
simple: They argued for hours without result.

example: His success is the result of many years of dedication and hard work.
example_cn: 他的成功，是多年投入與努力的成果。
pattern: N + be the result of + N
simple: The flood was the result of heavy rain.

chinese: 最終淨結果 / 總結結果
example: The net result of the tax changes was that most people paid more.
example_cn: 稅制調整的最終結果，就是大多數人繳了更多稅。
pattern: The net result of + N + be + that 子句
simple: The net result of his efforts was failure.

chinese: 讓某人進入 / 透露秘密給某人
example: I shouldn't have let him into the secret because he told everyone immediately.
example_cn: 我當初就不該把秘密告訴他，因為他馬上就跑去跟所有人說了。
simple: The bouncer let us into the club.

chinese: 減弱 / 停止 / 鬆勁
example: The heavy rain didn't let up all day, so the game had to be postponed.
example_cn: 大雨一整天都沒停，比賽只好延期。
simple: If the work doesn't let up soon, I'll be exhausted.

example: The price of gasoline has finally started to level off after months of rising.
example_cn: 汽油價格在連續漲了好幾個月之後，終於開始趨於平穩。
simple: The plane leveled off at 30,000 feet.

chinese: 對 (某人) 坦白 / 實話實說
example: I'll level with you: your project is currently far behind schedule.
example_cn: 我就跟你說實話吧：你的專案目前進度嚴重落後。
simple: She finally leveled with me about her finances.

example: We are aware of the difficult challenges that lie ahead of us in the new year.
example_cn: 我們很清楚，新的一年有許多艱難的挑戰在等著我們。
simple: A bright future lies ahead of you.

chinese: 是...的原因 / 隱藏在...背後
example: We need to find out what really lies behind her sudden decision to leave.
example_cn: 我們得搞清楚，她突然決定離開的真正原因是什麼。
simple: Hard work and luck lie behind his success.

chinese: 隱藏 / 保持低調 / 避風頭
example: The suspect decided to lie low for a while until the police stopped searching.
example_cn: 嫌犯決定先低調一陣子，等警方停止搜索再說。
simple: He's lying low until the scandal blows over.

example: You need to lighten up and stop worrying about every little detail!
example_cn: 你該放輕鬆一點，別再什麼小事都操心個沒完！
simple: Lighten up! It's just a game.

example: The number of people allowed in the building is limited to fifty for safety reasons.
example_cn: 基於安全考量，這棟建築物的人數上限是五十人。
simple: The offer is limited to new members.

chinese: (感覺、氣味、視線) 逗留 / 持續很久
example: The delicious smell of baking bread lingered on in the kitchen for hours.
example_cn: 烤麵包的香氣在廚房裡久久不散，持續了好幾個小時。
simple: Her eyes lingered on the photograph.

chinese: 連接 / 會合 / 與...取得聯繫
example: The two organizations decided to link up to increase their influence in the region.
example_cn: 這兩個組織決定聯手合作，以提升他們在該地區的影響力。
simple: We will link up with the main group later.

example: I suspect that someone was listening in on our private conversation.
example_cn: 我懷疑有人偷聽了我們的私人對話。
simple: They were caught listening in on the meeting.

chinese: 布滿 / 到處都是 (雜物或錯誤)
example: The beach was littered with plastic bottles and trash after the weekend.
example_cn: 週末過後，沙灘上到處都是寶特瓶和垃圾。
simple: His essay was littered with spelling errors.

chinese: 遵循 (原則) 生活 / 靠...維生
example: He is a man of high integrity who lives by a very strict moral code.
example_cn: 他是個品格高尚的人，嚴格遵守一套道德規範。
simple: They live by the sea.

chinese: 使人忘記 (過去的尷尬或錯誤)
example: He made a huge mistake at the office and thought he would never live it down.
example_cn: 他在辦公室犯了一個大錯，覺得這輩子都洗刷不了這個汙名。
pattern: S + will never live + it + down
simple: You'll never live down that silly costume.

example: It's time for him to get a real job instead of living off his parents.
example_cn: 他該去找份正經工作了，而不是一直靠父母養。
simple: Some animals live off insects.

chinese: 經歷過 (災難、困境) 並倖存
example: My grandfather lived through many difficult periods, including two world wars.
example_cn: 我祖父經歷過許多艱難時期，包括兩次世界大戰。
simple: They lived through the terrible earthquake.

chinese: 符合 / 達到 (期望、標準)
example: The new hotel didn't quite live up to the advertisements on its website.
example_cn: 這家新飯店並沒有達到網站廣告上說的那麼好。
simple: He tried to live up to his father's reputation.

example: The manager told the workers to stop loafing around and get back to work.
example_cn: 經理叫員工們別再混水摸魚，趕快回去工作。
simple: I spent the whole Sunday loafing around the house.

example: Activists are lobbying for stricter regulations on carbon emissions.
example_cn: 環保人士正在遊說，要求制定更嚴格的碳排放規範。
simple: They are lobbying the government against the new tax.

example: Valuable historical artifacts should be locked away to ensure their safety.
example_cn: 有價值的歷史文物應該鎖起來保管，以確保安全。
simple: The criminal was locked away for twenty years.

example: You need to log in to the system before you can access the files.
example_cn: 你需要先登入系統，才能存取那些檔案。
simple: Don't forget to log off before leaving.

chinese: 閒逛 / 遊蕩 (指在某處無所事事地逗留)
example: The security guards asked the groups of teenagers not to loiter around the entrance.
example_cn: 警衛要求那群青少年不要在入口處逗留。
simple: No loitering allowed near the bank.

chinese: 展望未來 / 預作打算
example: Successful entrepreneurs always look ahead to anticipate future trends.
example_cn: 成功的企業家總是放眼未來，預測市場趨勢。
simple: It's time to stop worrying and look ahead.

chinese: 授權用於 / 給予...執照
example: The software is licensed for educational use only, not for commercial profit.
example_cn: 這套軟體僅授權教育用途，不得用於商業營利。
simple: He is licensed for driving large trucks.

example: I love to lie on the grass and watch the white clouds in the blue sky.
example_cn: 我喜歡躺在草地上，看著藍天裡的白雲。
simple: The responsibility lies on your shoulders.

example: Please listen out for the doorbell while I am busy in the garden.
example_cn: 我在花園忙的時候，請你留意門鈴聲。
simple: I was listening out for his car.

chinese: 四下打量 / 尋找 / 參觀
example: We spent the whole morning looking around the ancient ruins of the castle.
example_cn: 我們花了一整個上午，在古城堡遺跡四處參觀。
simple: I'll look around and see if I can find my keys.

chinese: (對某人) 提出指控或批評 / 對準
example: Several serious accusations were leveled against the politician by his former assistant.
example_cn: 那位政治人物的前助理對他提出了幾項嚴重的指控。
simple: He leveled a gun at the target.

example: The dark clouds in the sky make it look like it's going to rain very soon.
example_cn: 天空烏雲密布，看起來很快就要下雨了。
simple: You look like your father.

chinese: 環顧 / 觀光 / 參觀
example: We spent the whole afternoon looking round the ancient temples in Kyoto.
example_cn: 我們整個下午都在京都逛那些古老的寺廟。
simple: I'd like to look round the house before buying it.

chinese: 讓...知情 / 納入溝通圈
example: Please loop me in on any further emails regarding the project deadline.
example_cn: 之後有關專案截止日期的郵件，請記得把我加入副本。
pattern: S + loop + 人 + in + on + N
simple: Make sure to loop the manager in.

example: Several valuable artifacts were looted from the museum during the riot.
example_cn: 暴動期間，博物館好幾件珍貴文物被搶走了。
pattern: S + be looted from + 地方
simple: Soldiers looted gold from the palace.

chinese: 輸掉 / 處於不利地位
example: Small independent bookstores often lose out to large online retailers.
example_cn: 小型獨立書店常常輸給大型線上零售商。
simple: Workers may lose out on overtime pay.

chinese: 悠閒地打發時間 / 懶洋洋地躺著
example: I love to lounge around the house in my pajamas on Sunday mornings.
example_cn: 我超愛星期天早上穿著睡衣在家裡閒晃。
simple: Stop lounging about and do some work!

chinese: 僥倖獲得 / 碰巧得到
example: He lucked into a very well-paid job just after he finished university.
example_cn: 他大學一畢業就運氣超好地找到一份高薪工作。
