simple: He chose to brave out the scandal.

example: The dog managed to break free from its leash and run after the cat.
example_cn: 那隻狗掙脫了牽繩，跑去追貓。
simple: The ship broke loose during the storm.

chinese: 折斷 / 中止 (談判/關係)
example: The two countries decided to break off diplomatic relations over the conflict.
example_cn: 這兩個國家因為衝突決定斷絕外交關係。
simple: He broke off a piece of chocolate.

example: The burglars used a crowbar to break open the safe in the jewelry store.
example_cn: 小偷用鐵撬把珠寶店的保險箱撬開了。
simple: She broke the coconut open with a hammer.

example: The doctor asked the patient to breathe in deeply so he could listen to her heart.
example_cn: 醫生請病人深呼吸，這樣他才能聽她的心跳。
simple: Breathe in through your nose.

example: I'll go and brew up a pot of fresh tea for our guests.
example_cn: 我去泡一壺新茶給客人喝。
simple: Trouble is brewing up in the city center.

chinese: 變明亮 / (心情)變好 / 使...更有活力
example: The new curtains really brighten up the entire living room.
example_cn: 新窗簾真的讓整個客廳亮起來了。
simple: The weather brightened up in the afternoon.

example: She was brimming over with excitement when she heard the good news.
example_cn: 她聽到這個好消息時，興奮得不得了。
pattern: S + be brimming over with + N
simple: The glass was brimming over with wine.

chinese: 帶領某人一起來 / 攜帶某物
example: Feel free to bring along a friend to the party on Friday night.
example_cn: 週五晚上的派對，歡迎你帶朋友一起來。
simple: Did you bring your camera along?

chinese: 使甦醒 / 說服 (改變心意) / 帶過來
example: It took a while to bring him around after he fainted in the crowded room.
example_cn: 他在擁擠的房間裡昏倒後，花了一陣子才把他弄醒。
simple: I finally brought her around to my point of view.

chinese: 把...帶過來 (通常指到別人家)
example: Why don't you bring over your new photos when you visit us next time?
example_cn: 下次你來拜訪時，要不要把你的新照片帶過來？
simple: He brought over some wine for the host.

example: I need to brush up on my English grammar before the proficiency test.
example_cn: 我需要在能力測驗前，好好複習一下英文文法。
pattern: S + (need to) brush up on + N
simple: You should brush up on your computer skills.

example: The small company eventually buckled under the pressure of the intense competition.
example_cn: 這家小公司最終在激烈的競爭壓力下撐不住了。
simple: He buckled under the weight of his responsibilities.

chinese: 使...變厚 / 使...顯得更大
example: He used several old newspapers to bulk out the empty spaces in the cardboard box.
example_cn: 他用幾張舊報紙塞滿紙箱裡的空隙。
simple: I added extra chapters to bulk out the book.

example: The noisy children were bundled off to bed so the adults could talk in peace.
example_cn: 吵鬧的孩子們被打發去睡覺，這樣大人才能安靜地聊天。
simple: They bundled him off to boarding school.

chinese: 逃學 / 曠工 (英式口語)
example: The two teenagers decided to bunk off school and go to the movies instead.
example_cn: 那兩個青少年決定翹課去看電影。
simple: He bunked off work to watch the game.

chinese: 使浮起 / 鼓舞 / 支撐
example: The success of the new product buoyed up the team's morale significantly.
example_cn: 新產品的成功大大提振了團隊的士氣。
simple: High demand buoyed up the oil prices.

chinese: 突然大喊 / 突然開始 (笑、哭)
example: Suddenly, he burst out laughing and nobody knew why it was so funny.
example_cn: 他突然大笑起來，沒人知道到底有什麼好笑的。
simple: She burst out crying after she heard the news.

chinese: 扣上鈕扣 / 保持沉默 (非正式)
example: Button up your jacket before you go outside; it's very cold.
example_cn: 出去前把外套扣好，外面很冷。
simple: You'd better button up if you want to keep your job.

example: The company plans to buy back its shares to increase their value.
example_cn: 公司計畫買回自己的股票，以提高它們的價值。
simple: He bought back his old house.

chinese: 大量買進 / 贊成 (計畫)
example: We need everyone to buy in to the new strategy for it to be successful.
example_cn: 要讓新策略成功，我們需要每個人都認同並支持。
simple: They bought in a large stock of food.

example: The criminal tried to buy off the witnesses, but they refused the money.
example_cn: 那個罪犯想收買證人，但他們拒絕收錢。
simple: You can't buy off the officials.

example: The larger company offered to buy out all the shareholders of the startup.
example_cn: 那家比較大的公司提出要收購這間新創公司的所有股東股份。
simple: He bought out his partner.

example: Investors began to buy up land in the region, expecting prices to rise.
example_cn: 投資人開始搶購這個地區的土地，預期價格會上漲。
simple: The bookstore bought up all the remaining copies.

chinese: 走開 / 滾開 (粗魯口語)
example: He told the annoying kids to buzz off because he was trying to work.
example_cn: 他跟那些煩人的小孩說「滾開」，因為他正在工作。
simple: I told him to buzz off.

example: The former star decided to cash in on his fame by writing a memoir.
example_cn: 那位前明星決定靠自己的名氣賺一筆，寫一本回憶錄。
simple: I need to cash in my savings bonds.

chinese: 解纜啟航 / 拋棄 (身分、責任)
example: The sailors were ready to cast off and begin their long journey across the Atlantic.
example_cn: 水手們準備解纜啟航，展開橫跨大西洋的漫長旅程。
simple: He decided to cast off his old identity.

example: The young man was cast out from his community because he broke the traditional rules.
example_cn: 那個年輕人因為違反傳統規範，被逐出了他的社群。
pattern: S + be cast out (from + 地方)
simple: Evil spirits were cast out.

chinese: 試圖抓住 / 渴望獲得
example: The drowning man tried to catch at a floating piece of wood.
example_cn: 溺水的人試圖抓住一塊漂浮的木頭。
simple: She caught at the chance to travel abroad.

example: The factory was ordered to cease from discharging waste into the river.
example_cn: 工廠被下令停止排放廢水到河裡。
simple: They finally ceased from fighting.

example: The owner had to chain up the dog at night to keep it from wandering off.
example_cn: 主人晚上得把狗拴起來，免得牠亂跑。
simple: The bike was chained up to a post.

example: The bull suddenly charged at the tourists who were trying to take photos.
example_cn: 公牛突然衝向那些正在拍照的觀光客。
simple: The soldiers charged at the enemy line.

example: We used a scarecrow to chase away the birds that were eating our seeds.
example_cn: 我們用稻草人來趕走那些吃種子的鳥。
simple: The sun soon chased away the morning mist.

chinese: (為了搭訕) 與某人閒談 / 套近乎
example: He spent the whole evening trying to chat up the girl sitting at the bar.
example_cn: 他花了一整個晚上試圖搭訕坐在吧檯邊的那個女生。
simple: He's always trying to chat up his colleagues.

chinese: 再回來查詢 / 重新核對
example: The manager asked me to check back in an hour to see if the files were ready.
example_cn: 經理要我一個小時後再回來確認檔案準備好了沒。
pattern: S + (need to) check back + 時間
simple: Please check back later for updates.

chinese: 在清單上勾選已完成項目
example: I checked off each item on the shopping list as I put it into my cart.
example_cn: 我把購物清單上的每一項物品放進推車時，就把它劃掉。
simple: He checked off the names on the attendance sheet.

chinese: 檢查...的情況 / 確認...是否沒事
example: The mother went upstairs to check on her sleeping baby.
example_cn: 媽媽上樓去查看她正在睡覺的寶寶。
simple: I'll go and check on the dinner.

example: You should check over your essay for any grammar mistakes before handing it in.
example_cn: 你應該在交作業前仔細檢查一遍有沒有文法錯誤。
simple: I checked through the documents to find the error.

chinese: 檢查 / 監督 / 調查狀況
example: The boss likes to check up on his employees several times a day.
example_cn: 老闆喜歡一天好幾次去監督他的員工。
simple: My parents are always checking up on me.

example: Thousands of fans gathered at the stadium to cheer on their favorite team.
example_cn: 數千名粉絲聚集在體育館，為他們最喜歡的隊伍加油打氣。
simple: The crowd cheered the runners on.

example: The manager really chewed out the staff for making such a costly mistake.
example_cn: 經理因為員工犯了這麼昂貴的錯誤，把他們狠狠罵了一頓。
simple: My dad chewed me out for staying out late.

chinese: 阻斷 / 限制 / 抑制
example: High interest rates might choke off the recent economic recovery.
example_cn: 高利率可能會扼殺近期的經濟復甦。
simple: The government tried to choke off the supply of illegal drugs.

example: She managed to choke out a few words of thanks before she started to cry.
example_cn: 她勉強擠出幾句感謝的話，然後就開始哭了。
simple: The weeds are choking out the flowers.

example: They had to chop down the old oak tree because it was diseased and dangerous.
example_cn: 他們不得不把那棵老橡樹砍掉，因為它生病了，很危險。
simple: Farmers chopped down the forest to create farmland.

example: Could you please help me chop up the onions for the salad?
example_cn: 可以幫我把洋蔥切碎，用來做沙拉嗎？
simple: Chop up the meat into small cubes.

chinese: 大快朵頤 / 開吃 (非正式)
example: After the long hike, everyone was ready to chow down on the delicious barbecue.
example_cn: 長途健行結束後，每個人都準備好要大快朵頤，享用美味的烤肉。
simple: They chowed down on pizza after the game.

chinese: 強力取締 / 嚴厲打擊
example: The police are planning to clamp down on speeding during the holiday weekend.
example_cn: 警方計畫在假期週末加強取締超速。
simple: The government is clamping down on tax evasion.

example: Please clear away the dishes after everyone has finished their dinner.
example_cn: 大家吃完晚餐後，請把碗盤收拾乾淨。
simple: Clear off! I'm trying to work.

example: The factory workers are required to clock in exactly at eight o'clock every morning.
example_cn: 工廠工人每天早上必須在八點整打卡上班。
simple: I usually clock off at five-thirty.

example: The street was closed off to traffic to allow the parade to pass safely.
example_cn: 那條街封閉了交通，讓遊行隊伍安全通過。
simple: The building was closed off after the gas leak.

chinese: 清倉 / 結束 (帳戶、計畫)
example: The store is holding a sale to close out its summer clothing collection.
example_cn: 這家店正在舉辦出清特賣，要把夏季服飾庫存清掉。
simple: He decided to close out his bank account.

example: The shopkeeper usually closes up the store around nine o'clock every night.
example_cn: 老闆通常每天晚上九點左右關店。
simple: The cut on my arm is starting to close up.

chinese: 提供訊息 / 使某人了解情況
example: Can you clue me in on what happened during the meeting while I was away?
example_cn: 可以跟我說一下，我不在的時候會議上發生了什麼事嗎？
simple: Make sure you clue the manager in.

example: The cells began to clump together under the microscope after the chemical was added.
example_cn: 加入化學藥劑後，細胞在顯微鏡下開始聚集成團。
simple: Snow was clumping together on the branches.

example: The teacher asked the students to collect up all the loose papers on the floor.
example_cn: 老師要學生把地上散落的紙張全部撿起來。
simple: I collected up my books and left.

example: The children spent the afternoon coloring in the pictures in their books.
example_cn: 孩子們整個下午都在幫書本裡的圖畫上色。
simple: She colored in the map with crayons.

chinese: 甦醒 / 順道拜訪 / 改變心意
example: He fainted during the hot afternoon, but he soon came around after drinking some water.
example_cn: 他在炎熱的下午昏倒了，但喝了些水後很快就恢復意識。
simple: Why don't you come around for coffee later?

chinese: 衝向 / 攻擊 / 處理(問題)
example: The stray dog suddenly came at me, barking loudly.
example_cn: 那隻流浪狗突然朝我衝過來，大聲吠叫。
simple: We need to come at this problem from a different angle.

chinese: 離開時帶著 (某種印象或結果)
example: I came away from the conference feeling very inspired by the speakers.
example_cn: 我離開會議時，覺得演講者讓我深受啟發。
pattern: S + come away (from + N) with + N
simple: The handle just came away in my hand.

chinese: 在...之前 / 提交...審理 / 比...更重要
example: In my life, the needs of my family always come before my career.
example_cn: 在我的人生中，家人的需求永遠比我的事業重要。
simple: The case will come before the court next month.

chinese: 下降 / (雨雪)落下 / 倒塌
example: The price of gasoline is expected to come down next month.
example_cn: 預計下個月汽油價格會下跌。
simple: The rain came down in torrents.

example: The police came for the suspect in the middle of the night.
example_cn: 警方在半夜來抓那名嫌疑人。
simple: I've come for my luggage.

chinese: 湧現 / 挺身而出 (提供資訊)
example: The police hope that more witnesses will come forth with information about the crime.
example_cn: 警方希望有更多目擊者出面提供與這起犯罪相關的資訊。
simple: New flowers come forth in spring.

chinese: 挺身而出 / 自告奮勇
example: A brave young man came forward to help the injured person after the accident.
example_cn: 事故發生後，一名勇敢的年輕人主動上前幫助傷者。
simple: No one came forward to claim the lost dog.

example: Nothing good will come of all this constant arguing within the team.
example_cn: 團隊裡一直吵個不停，根本不會有什麼好結果。
pattern: What + did + S + come of + N?
simple: He comes of a wealthy family.

example: She always comes out in a rash whenever she eats shellfish.
example_cn: 她每次吃貝類就會起疹子。
simple: The baby came out in red spots.

chinese: 安然度過 (難關) / (消息)傳來 / 成功辦好
example: The legal documents finally came through after months of waiting.
example_cn: 等了幾個月，法律文件終於下來了。
simple: She came through the operation successfully.

chinese: 偶然遇見 / 突然襲擊
example: While walking on the beach, we came upon a rare type of sea turtle.
example_cn: 我們在海灘上散步時，偶然發現了一種稀有的海龜。
simple: A sudden feeling of sadness came upon her.

example: The quality of this smartphone is excellent compared with other models in the same price range.
example_cn: 這款智慧型手機的品質，跟同價位的其他機型比起來，真的沒話說。
simple: Shakespeare compared the world to a stage.

example: Our school will compete against five other schools in the regional math competition.
example_cn: 我們學校將在區域數學競賽中，與其他五所學校一較高下。
simple: She is competing in a marathon tomorrow.

example: The committee is composed of experts from several different fields.
example_cn: 這個委員會由來自幾個不同領域的專家組成。
simple: Water is composed of hydrogen and oxygen.

example: The candidate was forced to concede to his opponent after the final votes were counted.
example_cn: 最後的票數統計出來後，這位候選人被迫向對手認輸。
simple: The government conceded to the protesters' demands.

example: This book concerns itself with the historical development of modern medicine.
example_cn: 這本書探討的是現代醫學的歷史發展。
simple: We are mainly concerned with the cost.

example: The guest speaker concluded her lecture with an inspiring quote from a famous author.
example_cn: 這位演講嘉賓最後引用了一句知名作家的名言，為她的演講畫下句點。
simple: The ceremony concluded with the national anthem.

chinese: 判處...刑罰 / 使遭受 (不幸)
example: The criminal was condemned to life imprisonment for his serious crimes.
example_cn: 這名罪犯因犯下重罪，被判處終身監禁。
simple: Many poor families are condemned to a life of poverty.

example: The editor asked the writer to condense the long story into a short article.
example_cn: 編輯要求作者把這篇長篇故事濃縮成一篇短文。
simple: Steam condenses into water.

example: His approval of the plan is conditioned on several major changes being made.
example_cn: 他對這個計畫的批准，是以必須做出幾項重大變更為前提。
simple: The offer is conditioned on a medical checkup.

example: The suspect finally confessed to the crime after hours of questioning.
example_cn: 經過好幾個小時的訊問，這名嫌犯終於坦承犯案。
simple: I must confess to being a little nervous.

example: Please confine your remarks to the topic of today's discussion.
example_cn: 請把你的發言範圍限制在今天討論的主題上。
simple: The patient was confined to bed for a week.

example: All new buildings must conform to the latest safety regulations.
example_cn: 所有新建築都必須符合最新的安全法規。
simple: The product does not conform to our standards.

chinese: 使面對 / 使面臨 (困難、證據)
example: The suspect was confronted with the evidence and finally admitted his guilt.
example_cn: 這名嫌犯在證據面前被當面對質，最後終於認罪。
simple: We were confronted with many obstacles during the project.

example: It is easy to confuse a mushroom with a poisonous toadstool if you are not careful.
example_cn: 如果不小心的話，很容易把蘑菇跟有毒的傘菌搞混。
simple: Don't confuse activity with progress.

chinese: (機器)故障 / (人)精疲力竭而睡著
example: The car engine suddenly conked out in the middle of the highway.
example_cn: 車子的引擎在高速公路中間突然熄火了。
simple: He conked out on the sofa after work.

example: The parents finally consented to their daughter's request to travel alone.
example_cn: 爸媽最後終於同意女兒自己一個人去旅行的要求。
simple: The director consented to an interview.

chinese: 交付 / 託付 / 淪落到
example: The old documents were consigned to the furnace to protect the secret.
example_cn: 那些舊文件被丟進火爐裡燒掉，以保護機密。
simple: She felt consigned to a life of poverty.

example: Her bright red dress contrasted sharply with the white snow in the background.
example_cn: 她那件亮紅色的洋裝，跟背景的白雪形成強烈對比。
simple: His actions contrast with his promises.

example: I managed to convince my parents of the benefits of gap years.
example_cn: 我成功說服爸媽，讓他們覺得空檔年其實有好處。
simple: He tried to convince them of his innocence.

example: Let the soup cool down for a few minutes before you eat it.
example_cn: 湯先放涼幾分鐘再喝。
simple: The argument ended when they both cooled down.

example: The teacher asked the students to copy out the poem from the whiteboard.
example_cn: 老師要學生把白板上的詩抄下來。
simple: He copied out the recipe onto a small card.

example: The figures in the report do not correspond to the actual sales data.
example_cn: 報告裡的數字跟實際的銷售數據對不上。
simple: I have been corresponding with him for many years.

chinese: 對...不利 / 算作對...不利的因素
example: Your lack of experience might count against you during the job interview.
example_cn: 你經驗不足，面試時可能會變成你的扣分項。
simple: His past record counted against him.

chinese: 算作...之一 / 列為其中
example: He is proud to count several famous artists among his close friends.
example_cn: 他很驕傲自己有好幾位知名藝術家當好朋友。
pattern: S + count + 人 + among + N (複數)
simple: I count you among my most loyal supporters.

example: The excited crowd began to count down the seconds to the new year.
example_cn: 興奮的人群開始倒數，迎接新年的到來。
simple: We are counting down the days to our holiday.

example: If you are planning a trip to the mountains, count me in!
example_cn: 如果你打算去山上玩，算我一份！
simple: I'm too tired for a movie, so count me out tonight.

example: The cashier counted up the total amount of money in the till at the end of the day.
example_cn: 店員在一天結束時，結算了收銀機裡的總金額。
simple: He counted up his winnings after the game.

example: They used a large tarp to cover over the equipment during the storm.
example_cn: 他們用一大塊防水布把設備蓋住，以防暴風雨。
simple: Snow covered over the footprints.

chinese: 取締 / 採取嚴厲打擊行動
example: The police are starting to crack down on speeding in the residential areas.
example_cn: 警方開始嚴厲取締住宅區的超速行為。
simple: The government is cracking down on tax evasion.

chinese: (因疲勞) 睡著 / 慘敗出局
example: I was so exhausted after the marathon that I crashed out on the sofa.
example_cn: 跑完馬拉松後我累翻了，直接在沙發上睡死。
simple: The team crashed out of the tournament in the first round.

chinese: 悄悄產生 / 悄悄接近
example: A sense of doubt began to creep in as the deadline approached.
example_cn: 隨著截止日期逼近，一股懷疑的感覺開始悄悄浮現。
simple: Old age is creeping up on me.

chinese: 劃掉 / 刪除 (清單項目)
example: I crossed the items off my shopping list as I put them in the cart.
example_cn: 我把購物清單上的東西放進推車時，就順手劃掉。
simple: Cross his name off the list.

chinese: 跨越 / 過渡 / (風格)轉型
example: The artist successfully crossed over from classical music to modern pop.
example_cn: 那位藝術家成功從古典樂跨界到現代流行樂。
pattern: S + cross over (from A to B)
simple: The bridge allows cars to cross over the river.

chinese: 取消約定 / 爽約 (英式口語)
example: We were supposed to go for dinner, but he cried off at the last minute.
example_cn: 我們本來要去吃晚餐，但他最後一刻臨時取消。
simple: I hope she doesn't cry off again.

example: The child cried out in pain when he fell off his bicycle.
example_cn: 那個小孩從腳踏車上摔下來時，痛得大叫。
simple: The building is crying out for a new coat of paint.

example: The hospital room was curtained off to provide more privacy for the patient.
example_cn: 醫院的病房拉上了簾子，讓病人更有隱私。
simple: They curtained off a small corner of the room.

chinese: 削減 / 減少 (支出、攝取)
example: The government is planning to cut back on defense spending next year.
example_cn: 政府打算明年削減國防開支。
pattern: S + (need to) cut back on + N
simple: I need to cut back on sugary drinks.

chinese: 輕點 / 輕抹 (通常指用布)
example: She used a handkerchief to dab at her eyes during the emotional scene.
example_cn: 她在情緒激動的場景中，用手帕輕輕按了按眼角。
simple: He dabbed at the blood on his forehead.

chinese: 以微弱稱讚來貶低 / 虛應故事地稱讚
example: The critic didn't like the movie and decided to damn it with faint praise.
example_cn: 那位影評人不喜歡這部電影，決定用明褒實貶的方式來批評它。
pattern: S + damn + N + with faint praise
simple: His review was just damning with faint praise.

example: Few people dare to speak out against the powerful leader.
example_cn: 很少有人敢公開反對那位強勢的領袖。
simple: He dared to challenge the champion.

example: The history of the ancient temple dates back to the twelfth century.
example_cn: 這座古廟的歷史可以追溯到十二世紀。
pattern: N + date(s) back to + 時間
simple: Our friendship dates back to high school.

chinese: 聲明反對 / 聲明支持
example: The majority of the council declared for the new environmental policy.
example_cn: 議會多數成員表態支持新的環保政策。
simple: He declared against the war.

example: They decided to decorate the living room with colorful flowers for the party.
example_cn: 他們決定用五彩繽紛的花卉來裝飾客廳，準備辦派對。
simple: The cake was decorated with fresh strawberries.

example: The company was forced to shut down after defaulting on its bank loans.
example_cn: 那家公司因為拖欠銀行貸款而被迫關門。
simple: If you default on your payments, your car will be taken.

example: Vaccines are used to defend the body from various types of infectious diseases.
example_cn: 疫苗用來保護身體免受各種傳染病的侵害。
simple: The walls defend the city against attacks.

chinese: 使偏離 / 轉移 (注意力)
example: The politician tried to deflect attention from the scandal by starting a new debate.
example_cn: 那位政治人物試圖發起新的辯論，來轉移大家對醜聞的注意力。
simple: The stone deflected the bullet from its path.

example: The manager needs to learn how to delegate tasks to his subordinates effectively.
example_cn: 經理需要學會如何有效地把任務分配給下屬。
simple: She delegated the decision to the committee.

chinese: 拯救 / 釋放 / 解脫
example: The prisoners hoped that a miracle would deliver them from their suffering.
example_cn: 囚犯們希望奇蹟能將他們從苦難中解救出來。
simple: Deliver us from evil.

chinese: 履行 (諾言) / 兌現 (計畫)
example: The company failed to deliver on its promise of providing better customer service.
example_cn: 那家公司未能兌現提供更好客戶服務的承諾。
simple: You must deliver on your commitments.

example: The captured suspect was delivered over to the local police station.
example_cn: 被捕的嫌犯被移交給了當地警察局。
pattern: S + deliver + N + over to + 單位
simple: The keys were delivered over to the new owner.

chinese: 向...要求 / 要求某人 (特質)
example: This complex project demands a lot of time and effort from all of us.
example_cn: 這個複雜的專案需要我們所有人投入大量的時間和精力。
simple: High concentration is demanded of students during exams.

example: I need to deposit this check in my bank account this afternoon.
example_cn: 我今天下午得把這張支票存進我的銀行帳戶。
pattern: S + deposit + N + in + 地方
simple: The river deposits silt in the valley.

example: The scientist believes that birds are descended from certain types of dinosaurs.
example_cn: 這位科學家認為鳥類是從某種恐龍演化而來的。
pattern: S + be descended from + 來源
simple: She is descended from a royal family.

chinese: 突然造訪 / 淪落到 (做壞事)
example: Thousands of fans descended on the city for the championship game.
example_cn: 成千上萬的球迷湧入這座城市，觀看冠軍賽。
simple: I never thought he would descend to cheating.

example: Most critics describe her latest novel as a true masterpiece of modern literature.
example_cn: 多數評論家認為她最新的小說是現代文學的真正傑作。
simple: He was described as a quiet man.

chinese: 從 (軍隊、組織) 逃跑 / 擅離職守
example: The soldier was severely punished after he tried to desert from the army.
example_cn: 那個士兵試圖逃兵後，被嚴厲處罰了。
simple: He deserted from his duties.

example: This new type of smartphone is specially designed for the elderly with larger icons.
example_cn: 這款新型智慧型手機是特別為長輩設計的，圖示更大。
simple: The course is designed for beginners.

example: The area around the lake has been designated as a nature reserve to protect wildlife.
example_cn: 湖區周圍已被劃定為自然保護區，用來保護野生動物。
simple: He was designated as the team leader.

type: 名詞 / 動詞 + 介系詞
example: Her strong desire for success kept her working late every night.
example_cn: 她對成功的強烈渴望，讓她每晚都工作到很晚。
simple: They expressed a desire for peace.

chinese: 停止 (做某事) / 斷念 (正式)
example: The court ordered the company to desist from using the illegal chemicals.
example_cn: 法院命令該公司停止使用那些非法化學物質。
simple: Please desist from smoking in this area.

example: From a young age, he seemed destined for a great career in music.
example_cn: 他從小就注定要在音樂領域大有可為。
simple: The cargo was destined for Singapore.

chinese: 派往 (執行任務) / 詳細說明
example: Several police officers were detailed to guard the entrance to the stadium.
example_cn: 好幾名警察被指派去守衛體育場的入口。
simple: He detailed his plans to the manager.

example: The doctor detected a slight irregularity in the patient's heartbeat.
example_cn: 醫生偵測到病人的心跳有輕微不規律。
simple: Pollutants were detected in the water.

chinese: 由...發展而來 / 起源於
example: Modern technology has developed from centuries of scientific research and discovery.
example_cn: 現代科技是經過數百年的科學研究與發現發展而來的。
simple: The illness developed from a simple infection.

example: What started as a small local business eventually developed into a global corporation.
example_cn: 一開始只是個在地小生意，最後卻發展成全球性企業。
simple: The argument developed into a serious fight.

chinese: 死於 (疾病、飢渴 / 傷口、意外)
example: In many developing countries, many children still die of preventable diseases.
example_cn: 在許多開發中國家，仍有不少兒童死於可預防的疾病。
simple: He died from his injuries sustained in the crash.

chinese: 逐漸消失 / 相繼死去
example: The sound of the train slowly died away as it moved further into the distance.
example_cn: 火車的聲音隨著它駛向遠方，漸漸消失了。
simple: The rare species is slowly dying off.

chinese: 在...方面不同 / 在...上持不同意見
example: The two candidates differ on several key issues regarding the national economy.
example_cn: 這兩位候選人在幾個關於國家經濟的關鍵議題上意見不同。
pattern: A and B differ in + N
simple: The brothers differ in temperament.

chinese: 開動 (吃飯) / 鑽研 / 深入調查
example: The detective decided to dig into the company's financial records to find the missing money.
example_cn: 偵探決定深入調查公司的財務紀錄，找出失蹤的錢。
simple: Dinner is served, so dig in!

example: The reporter managed to dig up some old scandals about the famous celebrity.
example_cn: 記者設法挖出了一些關於那位知名名人的舊醜聞。
simple: The dog dug up a bone in the backyard.

example: It is illegal to discriminate against people based on their race or gender.
example_cn: 基於種族或性別歧視他人是違法的。
simple: Can you discriminate between a real diamond and a fake?

chinese: 分發 / 隨便給予 / 端上 (菜餚)
example: The teacher dished out the test papers as soon as the bell rang.
example_cn: 老師一打鐘就把考卷發了下去。
simple: The chef is ready to dish up the main course.

example: It is sometimes difficult to distinguish the real painting from a clever copy.
example_cn: 有時候很難分辨真畫和仿得很像的贗品。
pattern: S + can distinguish A from B
simple: He can't distinguish between right and wrong.

example: The aid agencies are trying to distribute food and water to the earthquake victims.
example_cn: 救援機構正設法將食物和飲水分送給地震災民。
simple: The wealth was distributed among the citizens.

chinese: 在兩者間分配 / 在多人中分配 / 除以 (數學)
example: The inheritance will be divided between the two brothers as specified in the will.
example_cn: 遺產將按照遺囑的規定，由兩兄弟平分。
simple: Twelve divided by three is four.

example: The dishonest salesman tried to do the old woman out of her savings.
example_cn: 那個不老實的推銷員想騙走老太太的積蓄。
pattern: S + do + 人 + out of + N
simple: He felt he had been done out of a promotion.

chinese: 牽扯進 / 拖入 (麻煩、爭論)
example: I really don't want to be dragged into your argument with the manager.
example_cn: 我真的不想被你跟你經理的爭論拖下水。
simple: Please don't drag me into this mess.

example: I dreamed about my old elementary school teacher last night.
example_cn: 我昨晚夢見我小學的老師。
simple: She has always dreamed of becoming a professional dancer.

chinese: 趕走 / 擊退 / 驅逐
example: The loud noise from the alarm system was enough to drive away the burglars.
example_cn: 警報系統的巨大噪音足以嚇跑小偷。
simple: The invaders were driven out of the country.

chinese: 減少 / 落後 / 掉隊
example: The young runner began to drop behind the rest of the group in the final stage of the race.
example_cn: 那位年輕跑者在比賽的最後階段開始落後於其他人。
simple: Support for the policy is dropping away.

chinese: 擦乾 / 乾透 / 乾涸 / 枯竭
example: The small river completely dried up during the long summer drought.
example_cn: 那條小河在漫長的夏季乾旱期間完全乾涸了。
simple: Her creative ideas suddenly dried up.

chinese: 減輕 / 緩和 / 放鬆 (速度、壓力)
example: The heavy rain finally began to ease off around midnight.
example_cn: 大雨終於在午夜左右開始緩和下來。
simple: The boss should ease up on the new employees.

example: The engineers are trying to devise a new system for processing waste more efficiently.
example_cn: 工程師們正試圖設計一套新系統，更有效率地處理廢棄物。
simple: We need to devise a plan for the emergency.

chinese: 口授...給某人 / 向...發號施令
example: The manager was busy dictating a letter to his secretary when I entered.
example_cn: 我進去的時候，經理正忙著對秘書口述一封信。
simple: Stop trying to dictate to me!

chinese: 在家用餐 / 在外用餐
example: We usually dine out on Friday nights to celebrate the end of the work week.
example_cn: 我們通常在週五晚上外出用餐，慶祝一週工作結束。
simple: They dined out at a fancy French restaurant.

chinese: 繳械 / 消除 (疑慮、情緒)
example: The police officers managed to disarm the suspect of his weapon without any injuries.
example_cn: 警察們成功繳械了嫌疑犯的武器，沒有人受傷。
simple: Her warm smile disarmed him of his anger.

chinese: 與...脫離關係 / 劃清界線
example: The company tried to disassociate itself from the controversial comments made by the former employee.
example_cn: 公司試圖與前員工發表的爭議言論劃清界線。
simple: He disassociated himself from the group.

example: It is often hard for beginners to discern between different types of classical music.
example_cn: 初學者通常很難分辨不同類型的古典音樂。
pattern: S + discern between A and B
simple: He could barely discern between the two shades of green.

example: The details of the merger were not disclosed to the public until this morning.
example_cn: 合併案的細節直到今天早上才對外公開。
pattern: S + disclose + N + to + 人/公眾
simple: The source refused to disclose information to the police.

chinese: 從 (船、機) 下來 / 登陸
example: Passengers are requested to remain seated until the plane has fully stopped before disembarking.
example_cn: 請乘客留在座位上，直到飛機完全停穩後再下機。
simple: They disembarked from the cruise ship in the afternoon.

example: The athlete was disqualified from the race after failing a drug test.
example_cn: 那位運動員因為藥檢未過而被取消比賽資格。
simple: Poor eyesight may disqualify you from being a pilot.

chinese: 持異議 / 不同意 (官方觀點)
example: Two members of the committee dissented from the majority opinion on the issue.
example_cn: 委員會中有兩名成員對該議題的多數意見持反對立場。
simple: He dissented from the court's decision.

example: The politician tried to distance himself from the scandal to protect his reputation.
example_cn: 那位政治人物試圖與醜聞保持距離，以保護自己的名聲。
simple: She distanced herself from her former friends.

chinese: 剝奪 / 擺脫 / 賣掉 (股份)
example: The company was forced to divest itself of several smaller subsidiaries to avoid a monopoly.
example_cn: 該公司被迫出售幾家較小的子公司，以避免壟斷。
simple: He divested himself of all responsibilities.

chinese: 與...離婚 / 與...脫離 (現實)
example: His theories are completely divorced from reality and cannot be applied in practice.
example_cn: 他的理論完全脫離現實，無法應用在實務上。
simple: He was divorced from his wife last year.

chinese: 殺死 / 使筋疲力竭 (非正式)
example: The long marathon in the heat really did me in; I could barely walk afterward.
example_cn: 在炎熱天氣下跑馬拉松真的把我累垮了，之後幾乎走不動。
simple: The gang threatened to do him in.

chinese: 閃避 / 繞開 (人潮、問題)
example: He had to dodge around the pedestrians to reach the station on time.
example_cn: 他得在行人間閃來閃去，才能準時趕到車站。
simple: She dodged around the question during the interview.

example: The officer was drummed out of the army for his dishonorable conduct.
example_cn: 那名軍官因為不名譽的行為被開除軍籍。
pattern: S + be drummed out of + N
simple: He was drummed out of the club.

chinese: (開車) 換低檔 / 換高檔
example: You should change down to a lower gear before you start driving up the steep hill.
example_cn: 在開始爬陡坡之前，你應該先換到低檔。
simple: He changed up into fourth gear as he accelerated.

example: Please check over your application form one last time to ensure all information is correct.
example_cn: 請最後再檢查一次你的申請表，確保所有資料都正確無誤。
simple: The lawyer checked through the contract for any loopholes.

chinese: 檢查 / 監督 / 調查狀況
example: My parents often call me just to check up on how I am doing at university.
example_cn: 我父母常打電話來，只是想確認我在大學過得怎麼樣。
pattern: S + check up on + 人/物
simple: The manager likes to check up on the staff's progress.

chinese: 強力取締 / 嚴厲打擊
example: The local police are planning to clamp down on speeding in this residential area.
example_cn: 當地警方打算嚴格取締這個住宅區的超速行為。
simple: The government is clamping down on tax evasion.

example: They spent the entire afternoon cleaning down the kitchen walls before painting them.
example_cn: 他們花了整個下午把廚房牆壁徹底清潔乾淨，然後才上漆。
simple: Clean the surface down with a damp cloth.

example: Waiters quickly cleared away the empty plates once the guests finished eating.
example_cn: 客人一吃完，服務生就迅速把空盤子收走。
simple: Clear off! I'm trying to study.

example: Staff members are required to clock in before eight o'clock every morning.
example_cn: 員工必須每天早上八點前打卡上班。
simple: I usually clock off at five-thirty.

chinese: 甦醒 / 順道拜訪 / 改變心意
example: Why don't you come around for a cup of coffee this Saturday afternoon?
example_cn: 你這個週六下午要不要過來喝杯咖啡？
simple: He fainted but came round after a few minutes.

example: The poet compared his love to a red, red rose in his famous poem.
example_cn: 那位詩人在他的著名詩作中，把愛人比作一朵紅紅的玫瑰。
simple: This year's sales figures compare favorably with last year's.

example: Our school team will compete against five other schools in the regional basketball finals.
example_cn: 我們學校的隊伍將在區域籃球決賽中與其他五所學校競爭。
simple: She is competing against the best in the world.

example: The offer of the job is conditioned on your passing the medical examination.
example_cn: 這份工作的錄取條件是你要通過體檢。
simple: The agreement was conditioned on several factors.

chinese: 連結至 / 與...建立聯繫
example: Social media helps people connect with long-lost friends from their childhood.
example_cn: 社群媒體幫助人們與童年失聯的老朋友重新聯繫上。
simple: Is the printer connected to the network?

example: The descriptions in the catalog do not always correspond to the actual products.
example_cn: 目錄上的描述不一定跟實際產品相符。
simple: I have been corresponding with him for years.

chinese: 算我一個 / 排除在外
example: If you are planning a trip to the beach this weekend, please count me in!
example_cn: 如果你這週末打算去海邊玩，記得算我一份！
simple: I'm too tired to go, so count me out tonight.

example: The history of this ancient temple dates back to the twelfth century.
example_cn: 這座古廟的歷史可以追溯到十二世紀。
pattern: N + date(s) back to + 時間
simple: Our friendship dates back to high school.

chinese: 聲明反對 / 聲明支持
example: The majority of the committee members declared for the new proposal.
example_cn: 委員會的多數成員都表態支持這項新提案。
simple: He declared against the war.

example: The new laws are designed to defend consumers from unfair business practices.
example_cn: 這些新法規旨在保護消費者免受不公平商業行為的侵害。
simple: The wall defended the town from attack.

chinese: 向...要求 / 要求某人 (具備特質)
example: The manager demands a high level of dedication from all of his employees.
example_cn: 經理要求所有員工都要有高度的敬業精神。
simple: High concentration is demanded of students during exams.

chinese: 突然造訪 (湧向) / 淪落到...
example: Thousands of fans descended on the city for the championship match.
example_cn: 成千上萬的粉絲湧入這座城市，只為了觀看冠軍賽。
simple: I never thought he would descend to cheating.

example: After much discussion, the board determined on a plan to expand the business.
example_cn: 經過多次討論，董事會決定了一個擴展業務的計畫。
simple: She determined on a career in law.

chinese: 逐漸消失 / 相繼死去
example: The sound of the train slowly died away as it moved further into the distance.
example_cn: 火車的聲音隨著它逐漸駛向遠方而慢慢消失。
simple: The rare species is slowly dying off.

example: Many soldiers were willing to die for their country during the war.
example_cn: 戰爭期間，許多士兵願意為國家犧牲生命。
simple: I am dying for a cold drink.

chinese: 死於 (疾病、飢渴 / 傷口、意外)
example: In many developing countries, people still die of preventable diseases.
example_cn: 在許多開發中國家，人們仍然死於可預防的疾病。
simple: He died from his injuries.

chinese: 在...方面不同 / 持不同意見
example: The two candidates differ on several key issues regarding the economy.
example_cn: 這兩位候選人在經濟相關的幾個關鍵議題上意見不同。
pattern: A and B differ in/on + N
simple: The brothers differ in temperament.

chinese: 開動 (大吃) / 鑽研 / 找出
example: The researcher decided to dig into the old archives to find the missing facts.
example_cn: 研究人員決定深入挖掘舊檔案，以找出遺失的事實。
simple: Dinner is ready, so dig in!

example: We usually dine out on Friday nights to celebrate the end of the week.
example_cn: 我們通常在週五晚上外出用餐，慶祝一週的結束。
simple: They dined out at a French restaurant.

example: The security guard directed the visitors to the main conference hall.
example_cn: 警衛引導訪客前往主要會議廳。
pattern: S + direct + 人 + to + 地方
simple: Could you direct me to the station?

example: The manager was disappointed in the team's poor performance during the match.
example_cn: 經理對球隊在比賽中的糟糕表現感到失望。
pattern: S + be disappointed in + 人/物
simple: I was disappointed in his reaction.

example: He finally discovered his true identity to his long-lost relatives.
example_cn: 他終於向失散多年的親人揭露了自己的真實身份。
simple: The secret was discovered to the world.

example: I need to discuss the new project details with my manager before we start.
example_cn: 在開始之前，我需要和經理討論新專案的細節。
simple: We will discuss the issue with the experts.

example: The two neighbors had a long dispute about the exact location of the property line.
example_cn: 兩位鄰居為了地界線的準確位置爭論了很久。
simple: There is no dispute about the facts of the case.

example: The aid agency began to distribute food and water among the flood victims.
example_cn: 救援機構開始向水災災民分發食物和飲水。
pattern: S + distribute + N + among + N(複數)
simple: The profits were distributed among the shareholders.

example: There is a growing distrust of mainstream media among younger generations.
example_cn: 年輕世代對主流媒體的不信任感日益增加。
pattern: S + have a deep distrust of + N
simple: His distrust of strangers makes him very lonely.

chinese: 偏離 / 背離 / (意見)分歧
example: The new model's performance slightly diverges from the results we expected.
example_cn: 新型號的表現與我們預期的結果有些微差異。
simple: Their paths diverged from each other after high school.

example: The loud noise outside diverted my attention from the difficult exam paper.
example_cn: 外面的大噪音分散了我對困難考卷的注意力。
simple: The flight was diverted from its original route.

chinese: 幫...忙 / 壞了...的事 / 毀掉
example: What can I do for you today? Do you need help with anything?
example_cn: 今天有什麼我能為你服務的嗎？需要幫忙什麼嗎？
simple: The scandal has done for his political career.

chinese: 徵召入伍 / 抽調進入
example: Thousands of young men were drafted into the army during the war.
example_cn: 戰爭期間，成千上萬的年輕男子被徵召入伍。
simple: He was drafted into the national team.

example: The manager drew me aside after the meeting to discuss my performance.
example_cn: 會議結束後，經理把我拉到一旁討論我的表現。
simple: She drew him aside to tell him the secret.

chinese: 消耗 / 減少 / 引起 (批評)
example: The country had to draw down its oil reserves to deal with the energy crisis.
example_cn: 這個國家不得不動用石油儲備來應對能源危機。
simple: His comments drew down a lot of criticism.

chinese: 吸入 / (天)黑得早 / 捲入
example: As autumn approaches, the days start to draw in and it gets dark much earlier.
example_cn: 隨著秋天來臨，白天開始變短，天黑得比以前早很多。
simple: The station was drawn in by the heavy fog.

example: I really don't want to be drawn into your argument with the manager.
example_cn: 我真的不想被捲入你和經理的那場爭論。
simple: The country was drawn into a costly war.

chinese: 抽走 (液體) / 轉移 (注意力)
example: The mechanic used a pump to draw off the excess oil from the engine.
example_cn: 技師用幫浦把引擎裡多餘的機油抽出來。
simple: They tried to draw off the enemy's attention.

example: I dreamed about my old high school teacher last night; it was so strange.
example_cn: 我昨晚夢到高中的老老師，真的超奇怪。
simple: She has always dreamed of becoming a famous actress.

example: He was so thirsty after the hike that he drank down the whole bottle of water in one go.
example_cn: 他健行完口渴得要命，一口氣就把整瓶水喝光了。
simple: Drink down your medicine.

example: Let's all stand up and drink to the success of the new project!
example_cn: 我們大家一起站起來，為新專案的成功乾杯！
simple: We drank to her health and happiness.

chinese: 趕走 / 擊退 / 驅逐
example: The loud noise from the alarm was enough to drive away the burglars.
example_cn: 警報器的巨大聲響足夠把闖空門的小偷嚇跑。
simple: The army managed to drive back the invaders.

example: I might drop around this evening to return the books I borrowed.
example_cn: 我可能會今晚順道過去還我借的書。
simple: Feel free to drop in anytime.

chinese: 減少 / 消散 / 脫落
example: Support for the new policy began to drop away as the costs increased.
example_cn: 隨著成本增加，對新政策的支持開始逐漸減少。
simple: The cliff drops away steeply to the sea.

example: The runner began to drop back as the rest of the group speeded up.
example_cn: 當其他人加速時，那位跑者開始落後。
simple: I dropped back to talk to my friend.

chinese: 淹沒在...之中 / 沉溺於
example: The students felt they were drowning in a sea of paperwork before the final exams.
example_cn: 學生們覺得期末考前簡直被一堆作業淹沒了。
simple: She was drowning in her own sorrows.

chinese: 變暗 / 變淡 / 使遲鈍
example: The bright colors of the painting have dulled down over many years.
example_cn: 這幅畫鮮豔的顏色經過多年已經變得暗淡。
simple: Wait for the noise to dull down.

chinese: 減輕 / 緩和 / 放鬆 (速度、壓力)
example: The heavy rain finally began to ease off around midnight.
example_cn: 大雨終於在午夜左右開始緩和下來。
simple: You should ease up on the new staff.

example: We decided to eat in tonight instead of going to a restaurant.
example_cn: 我們決定今晚在家吃，不去餐廳了。
simple: Let's eat in and save some money.

example: As the minutes passed, our hopes of winning the match began to ebb away.
example_cn: 隨著時間一分一秒過去，我們贏得比賽的希望開始逐漸消退。
simple: The patient's life was ebbing away.

chinese: 刪除 / 剪掉 (不必要的部分)
example: The scene was edited out of the final version of the movie to save time.
example_cn: 為了節省時間，那個場景從電影的最終版本中被剪掉了。
simple: The reporter edited out the repetitive parts.

example: She elected to take an early retirement to spend more time with her grandchildren.
example_cn: 她選擇提早退休，以便有更多時間陪孫子孫女。
simple: They elected to remain silent.

chinese: 著手進行 / 開始 (新旅程或計畫)
example: The young entrepreneur is ready to embark on a new business venture.
example_cn: 那位年輕的企業家準備好要展開一項新的商業冒險。
simple: She embarked on a career in politics.

example: Many families decided to emigrate to Canada in search of better job opportunities.
example_cn: 很多家庭決定移民到加拿大，尋找更好的工作機會。
simple: They emigrated to Australia last year.

example: He is currently employed in the research and development department of a tech giant.
example_cn: 他目前在一家科技巨頭的研發部門工作。
simple: She is employed in the tourism industry.

example: The new law empowers the local government to take action against pollution.
example_cn: 這項新法賦予地方政府權力，可以對污染問題採取行動。
simple: Education empowers people to change their lives.

example: Please empty out your pockets before you put your pants in the washing machine.
example_cn: 請把口袋裡的東西清空，再把褲子放進洗衣機。
simple: He emptied out his backpack.

example: Please enclose a copy of your ID in the application envelope.
example_cn: 請在申請信封內附上一張身分證影本。
simple: The garden is enclosed in a high wall.

example: The data is encoded in a specific format to ensure its security during transmission.
example_cn: 資料會以特定格式編碼，確保傳輸過程中的安全性。
simple: The message was encoded in binary.

chinese: 侵犯 / 侵占 (權利、時間)
example: He felt that his work was starting to encroach on his personal family time.
example_cn: 他覺得工作開始壓縮到他的個人家庭時間。
simple: The city is encroaching on the forest.

example: You need to endorse your signature on the back of the check to deposit it.
example_cn: 你必須在支票背面簽名背書，才能存入銀行。
pattern: S + endorse + N + on + 地方
simple: The license was endorsed on the back.

chinese: 與...交流 / 交戰 / 接合
example: It is important for teachers to engage with their students to create a better learning environment.
example_cn: 老師和學生互動很重要，這樣才能創造更好的學習環境。
simple: The gears fail to engage with each other.

example: The professor decided to enlarge on the theory during the second half of the lecture.
example_cn: 教授決定在講座的下半場進一步闡述這個理論。
simple: Could you enlarge on your original suggestion?

example: He decided to enlist in the navy after graduating from high school.
example_cn: 他高中畢業後決定加入海軍。
simple: They enlisted in the local charity project.

chinese: 使豐富 / 添加...使肥沃
example: The museum decided to enrich its collection with several modern artworks.
example_cn: 博物館決定添購幾件現代藝術作品，豐富館藏。
simple: The soil was enriched with organic fertilizer.

example: After graduation, he entered on a new stage of his life as a professional engineer.
example_cn: 畢業後，他以專業工程師的身分展開了人生的新階段。
simple: The project has entered on its final phase.

example: The host entertained the guests with several interesting stories about his travels.
example_cn: 主人用幾個有趣的旅遊故事來招待客人。
simple: She entertained the children with magic tricks.

example: The critics enthused about the young actor's outstanding performance in the new movie.
example_cn: 影評人對這位年輕演員在新片中的出色表現讚不絕口。
simple: Everyone was enthusing about the holiday plans.

example: The manager entrusted the keys to his most reliable assistant.
example_cn: 經理把鑰匙交給他最信任的助理保管。
simple: I was entrusted with the important task.

example: The mountain peak was completely enveloped in heavy fog this morning.
example_cn: 今天早上山頂完全被濃霧籠罩。
simple: She was enveloped in a warm blanket.

example: The coastline is slowly being eroded away by the constant action of the waves.
example_cn: 海岸線正被海浪持續侵蝕，慢慢消退。
simple: Inflation is eroding away people's savings.

example: Security guards will escort visitors to the main conference hall.
example_cn: 保全人員會護送訪客到主要會議廳。
pattern: S + escort + 人 + to + 地方
simple: The police escorted the prisoner to the court.

example: The company was established in Taipei over fifty years ago.
example_cn: 這家公司五十多年前在台北成立。
simple: He eventually established himself in the legal profession.

example: The total damage from the flood is estimated at several million dollars.
example_cn: 這場洪水造成的總損失估計有幾百萬美元。
pattern: N + be estimated at + 數量
simple: The crowd was estimated at 5,000 people.

example: The events of that day are permanently etched into her memory.
example_cn: 那天發生的事已經深深烙印在她的記憶裡。
simple: Names were etched into the glass.

example: The water slowly evaporated from the puddle as the sun came out.
example_cn: 太陽出來後，水坑裡的水慢慢蒸發掉了。
simple: Our excitement began to evaporate from the room.

chinese: 使平穩 / 使相等 / 扯平
example: The difference in temperature between day and night evens out in the summer.
example_cn: 夏天時，白天和晚上的溫差會變得比較平均。
simple: They evened up the score in the final minute.

example: The family was evicted from their apartment after failing to pay the rent.
example_cn: 這家人因為付不出房租，被從公寓裡趕了出來。
simple: The squatters were evicted from the building.

example: Modern technology has evolved from simple tools used by our ancestors.
example_cn: 現代科技是從我們祖先使用的簡單工具演變而來的。
simple: The simple idea evolved into a massive project.

chinese: 強求 / 勒索 / 榨取
example: The kidnappers tried to exact a large ransom from the wealthy family.
example_cn: 綁匪試圖向那個有錢家庭勒索一大筆贖金。
simple: The teacher exacted strict discipline from his students.

example: This year's profits exceeded our expectations by twenty percent.
example_cn: 今年的利潤比我們預期的高出了百分之二十。
pattern: N + exceed + N + by + 數字
simple: The speed limit was exceeded by 10 miles per hour.

example: She has always excelled in mathematics and science since a young age.
example_cn: 她從小在數學和科學方面就一直表現得很出色。
simple: He excels at playing the piano.

example: New employees are sometimes excepted from certain company requirements during training.
example_cn: 新進員工在訓練期間，有時可以免於遵守公司的某些規定。
simple: No one is excepted from the law.

example: I'd like to exchange this shirt for a larger size, please.
example_cn: 我想把這件襯衫換成大一點的尺寸，麻煩了。
simple: They exchanged their goods for food.

example: He was excluded from the meeting because he was no longer a board member.
example_cn: 他因為不再是董事會成員，所以被排除在會議之外。
simple: Don't exclude him from your plans.

example: There is no excuse for such rude behavior in a professional workplace.
example_cn: 在專業的職場環境裡，沒有理由可以為這種粗魯的行為開脫。
simple: She was excused from gym class due to her injury.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: Writing an essay is a good exercise in organizing your thoughts logically.
example_cn: 寫文章是訓練邏輯思考能力的好方法。
pattern: N + is an exercise in + N/V-ing
simple: The project was an exercise in patience.

example: Parents can exert a strong influence on their children's career choices.
example_cn: 父母對小孩的職業選擇可以產生很大的影響。
pattern: S + exert + N + on + 人/物
simple: The atmosphere exerts pressure on us.

chinese: 從...排出 / 因...而筋疲力竭
example: She was completely exhausted from working twelve hours a day.
example_cn: 她每天工作十二個小時，累得筋疲力盡。
simple: Fumes are exhausted from the pipe.

example: The artist will exhibit her latest paintings at the national gallery next month.
example_cn: 這位藝術家下個月將在國家美術館展出她最新的畫作。
pattern: S + exhibit + N + at + 地方
simple: Many companies exhibit at the trade fair.

example: The former king was exiled to a remote island after the revolution.
example_cn: 革命之後，前任國王被流放到一個偏遠的小島上。
pattern: S + be exiled to + 地方
simple: Napoleon was exiled to St. Helena.

example: In some poor regions, people still have to exist on less than two dollars a day.
example_cn: 在一些貧困地區，人們仍然得靠每天不到兩美元過活。
simple: Some desert animals exist on very little water.

example: The company decided to exit from the European market due to low sales.
example_cn: 這家公司因為銷售業績不佳，決定退出歐洲市場。
simple: Please exit from the rear door.

chinese: 證明...無罪 / 免除 (責任)
example: New DNA evidence was used to exonerate the man from the crime he didn't commit.
example_cn: 新的DNA證據用來證明這名男子沒有犯下他沒做過的罪行。
simple: The report exonerated him from all blame.

example: The student was expelled from school for repeated violations of the safety rules.
example_cn: 這名學生因為多次違反安全規定被學校開除。
simple: He was expelled from the club for his rude behavior.

example: The company expended a huge amount of resources on the research of new energy.
example_cn: 這間公司在新能源研究上投入了大量資源。
simple: Too much energy was expended on minor details.

chinese: 流傳 / 漂浮 / 閒晃
example: Several rumors have been floating about regarding the possible closure of the factory.
example_cn: 關於工廠可能關閉的傳言已經流傳好一陣子了。
simple: There's an idea floating about in my head.

chinese: 不受...影響 / 沒有... (指負面事物)
example: The new system is designed to be free from errors and easy for everyone to use.
example_cn: 這個新系統設計成零錯誤，而且每個人都能輕鬆使用。
simple: Finally, the region is free from war.

chinese: 不含...的 / 免於... (指成分、義務)
example: This bottle of water is guaranteed to be free of any artificial additives.
example_cn: 這瓶水保證不含任何人工添加物。
simple: The goods were delivered free of charge.

example: He felt that his colleagues were trying to freeze him out of the new project.
example_cn: 他覺得同事們試圖把他排擠出這個新專案。
simple: Small shops are being frozen out by big supermarkets.

chinese: 充滿 (通常指沉重的負擔或情感)
example: The atmosphere in the room was freighted with tension as the results were announced.
example_cn: 結果公布時，房間裡的氣氛充滿了緊張。
simple: Her voice was freighted with sadness.

chinese: 充當...的掩護 / 做為...的代理人
example: The travel agency was actually a front for an illegal gambling operation.
example_cn: 這間旅行社其實是非法賭博活動的掩護。
pattern: N + serves as a front for + N
simple: He was accused of fronting for a criminal gang.

chinese: 面向 / 正對著 (街道、河流)
example: The beautiful old hotel fronts onto the main square of the city.
example_cn: 這棟漂亮的老飯店正面對著城市的主要廣場。
simple: The garden fronts onto a quiet river.

example: The car windows frosted over during the extremely cold night.
example_cn: 在極度寒冷的夜晚，車窗結了一層霜。
simple: I couldn't see out because the glass had frosted up.

example: She fumbled in her bag for her keys but couldn't find them in the dark.
example_cn: 她在包包裡摸索鑰匙，但黑暗中怎麼也找不到。
simple: He fumbled about in the drawer for a pen.

example: The manager was fuming about the delay in the delivery of the materials.
example_cn: 經理因為材料運送延誤而氣得火冒三丈。
simple: She sat there fuming at his rudeness.

example: The police asked the witness to furnish them with a detailed description of the suspect.
example_cn: 警方要求目擊者提供嫌疑人的詳細描述。
simple: The room was furnished with a desk and a chair.

chinese: 逼近 / 趕上 (競爭對手)
example: The runner was gaining on the leader as they approached the finish line.
example_cn: 跑者在接近終點線時逐漸追上領先者。
pattern: S + be gaining on + 人/物
simple: Our company is gaining on the market leader.

example: The politician managed to gain over several opponents to his side.
example_cn: 這位政治人物成功說服了幾位對手站到他這邊。
simple: We need to gain over the undecided voters.

chinese: 草草完成 / 飛快進行
example: The teacher had to gallop through the last chapter to finish the syllabus on time.
example_cn: 老師為了按時教完課程大綱，只好匆匆帶過最後一章。
simple: Don't gallop through your presentation; speak slowly.

example: The company decided to gamble on the new technology, hoping it would be a success.
example_cn: 公司決定賭一把新技術，希望它能成功。
simple: I wouldn't gamble on the weather staying clear.

example: Most of the information in the report was garnered from reliable public sources.
example_cn: 報告中的大部分資訊都是從可靠的公開來源收集而來的。
pattern: S + be garnered from + 來源
simple: She garnered support from her colleagues.

example: The chef garnished the soup with fresh herbs to enhance its flavor.
example_cn: 主廚在湯上撒了新鮮香草來增添風味。
simple: The salad was garnished with nuts.

example: The children gathered around the storyteller to hear about the magical forest.
example_cn: 孩子們圍在說故事的人身邊，想聽聽關於魔法森林的故事。
simple: The fans gathered around the famous actor.

example: I gather from your expression that you are not happy with my decision.
example_cn: 從你的表情來看，我猜你對我的決定不太滿意。
pattern: S + gather from + N + that 子句
simple: As far as I can gather from the news, the situation is stable.

example: Farmers were busy gathering in the wheat before the storm arrived.
example_cn: 農夫們趕在暴風雨來臨前，忙著把小麥收割起來。
simple: The dress was gathered in at the waist.

example: They sat in silence, gazing on the moon as it rose over the mountains.
example_cn: 他們靜靜坐著，凝視著月亮從山巒升起。
simple: He gazed on her with admiration.

example: The training program is geared to the specific needs of new employees.
example_cn: 這個培訓課程是針對新進員工的特定需求量身打造的。
simple: The course is geared to beginners.

chinese: 研讀 / 收集詳細資訊 (英式口語)
example: I need to gen up on the company's history before my interview tomorrow.
example_cn: 我需要在明天面試前，好好惡補一下公司的歷史。
simple: Have you genned up on the new regulations?

example: Electricity in this region is primarily generated from renewable sources like wind and solar power.
example_cn: 這個地區的電力主要來自風力和太陽能等再生能源。
simple: Heat is generated from the friction of the two surfaces.

chinese: 四處走動 / 傳播 (消息)
example: Despite her old age, she still manages to get about the city using public transport.
example_cn: 儘管她年紀大了，還是能靠大眾運輸工具在城裡到處走動。
simple: News gets about quickly in a small village.

chinese: 變得自命不凡 / 變得傲慢
example: Ever since he was promoted to manager, he has started to get above himself.
example_cn: 自從他升上經理後，就開始變得有點自以為是。
simple: Don't get above yourself just because you won one game.

chinese: 追趕 / 催促 / 批評
example: The teacher had to get after the students for not submitting their homework on time.
example_cn: 老師得去盯那些沒按時交作業的學生。
simple: He got after the thief down the street.

chinese: 重新開始 (習慣或興趣) / 回到
example: I really want to get back into swimming once the weather becomes warmer.
example_cn: 等天氣變暖，我很想重新開始游泳。
simple: It took him a while to get back into the routine of work.

chinese: 批評某人 / 找某人麻煩
example: Don't be so hard on yourself; there's no reason to get down on yourself for one mistake.
example_cn: 別對自己這麼嚴格，沒必要因為一個錯誤就自責。
simple: The boss is always getting down on him for minor errors.

chinese: 參加 / 分一杯羹 (通常指獲利或秘密活動)
example: Everyone wants to get in on the new investment opportunity before the deadline.
example_cn: 每個人都想在截止日前，參與這個新的投資機會。
pattern: S + want to get in on + N
simple: I'd like to get in on the discussion.

chinese: 到達 / 開始 / 使某人煩惱
example: The constant noise from the construction site is finally starting to get to me.
example_cn: 工地傳來的持續噪音，終於開始讓我受不了了。
simple: What time will you get to the station?

chinese: 從事 (尤指調皮的事) / 達到
example: I wonder what the children are getting up to in the garden; it's too quiet.
example_cn: 我在想孩子們在花園裡搞什麼鬼，安靜得太不尋常了。
pattern: What + did + S + get up to?
simple: The temperature got up to forty degrees.

example: She was gifted with an extraordinary voice that touched everyone's heart.
example_cn: 她天生擁有一副動人的嗓音，感動了每個人的心。
simple: He is gifted with a talent for mathematics.

example: The students couldn't help but giggle at the teacher's funny tie.
example_cn: 學生們看到老師那條好笑的領帶，忍不住咯咯笑了起來。
simple: Stop giggling at your sister.

chinese: 為...做好準備 (特別指困難或挑戰)
example: The company is girding for a long legal battle over the patent rights.
example_cn: 公司正準備為專利權打一場長期的法律戰。
simple: They girded themselves for the storm.

chinese: 慷慨奉獻 (時間、金錢、自己)
example: He always gives of his time to help younger students with their research.
example_cn: 他總是樂於付出時間，幫助學弟妹做研究。
simple: She gave generously of her own money to the charity.

example: The large windows in the bedroom give onto a beautiful balcony overlooking the sea.
example_cn: 臥室裡的大窗戶通向一個可以眺望大海的美麗陽台。
pattern: N (門/窗) + give(s) onto + 地方
simple: The kitchen door gives onto the garden.

example: The bullet glanced off the metal plate and hit the wall instead.
example_cn: 子彈擦過金屬板，反而打中了牆壁。
simple: The ball glanced off his head into the goal.

example: Could you just glance over this document and tell me if it looks okay?
example_cn: 你能不能快速掃過這份文件，跟我說看起來有沒有問題？
simple: He glanced through the newspaper while waiting.

example: The librarian glared at the noisy students until they became silent.
example_cn: 圖書館員瞪著吵鬧的學生，直到他們安靜下來。
simple: She glared at him in anger.

example: Her eyes gleamed with excitement as she talked about her upcoming trip to Paris.
example_cn: 她談到即將去巴黎的旅行時，眼睛閃爍著興奮的光芒。
simple: The polished surface gleamed with light.

chinese: 從...中辛苦搜集 (資訊、知識)
example: Most of the information in the report was gleaned from public records.
example_cn: 報告裡的大部分資訊都是從公開紀錄中蒐集來的。
pattern: S + glean + N + from + 來源
simple: What secrets did you glean from his diary?

example: The elegant swan glided into the middle of the lake without making a ripple.
example_cn: 優雅的天鵝滑進湖中央，沒有激起一絲漣漪。
simple: The car glided into the garage.

chinese: 得意揚揚地看著 / 幸災樂禍
example: It's not polite to gloat over your victory while your opponent is disappointed.
example_cn: 在對手失望的時候，還為自己的勝利沾沾自喜，這樣不太禮貌。
simple: He gloated over her failure.

chinese: 以此為榮 / 沉浸在...的快樂中
example: The fans gloried in their team's unexpected championship victory.
example_cn: 粉絲們為球隊出乎意料地奪冠而欣喜若狂。
simple: She gloried in her new fame.

chinese: (臉、身體) 洋溢著 / 閃耀著 (健康或情緒)
example: The little girl's face was glowing with health after her summer holiday at the beach.
example_cn: 小女孩在海邊度過暑假後，臉色紅潤，充滿健康光采。
simple: His face glowed with pride as he received the award.

chinese: 黏在...上 / 目不轉睛地看著
example: The children were glued to the television screen during the entire cartoon movie.
example_cn: 整部卡通電影播放期間，孩子們都緊盯著電視螢幕。
simple: He glued the label to the box.

chinese: 咬掉 / (比喻) 侵蝕、折磨
example: Doubts about the decision began to gnaw away at his confidence.
example_cn: 對這個決定的疑慮開始侵蝕他的信心。
simple: The mouse was gnawing away at the cheese.

example: We need to go across the bridge to get to the other side of the river.
example_cn: 我們需要過橋才能到河的對岸。
simple: Let's go across the street to the cafe.

chinese: 沿著...走 / 進展 / 贊同
example: As you go along the path, you will see a small lake on your right.
example_cn: 沿著這條路走，你會看到右手邊有一個小湖。
simple: How is your project going along?

example: Red wine and steak usually go together perfectly in a traditional dinner.
example_cn: 在傳統晚餐中，紅酒和牛排通常是很完美的搭配。
simple: Success and hard work often go together.

chinese: 閒聊關於... / 說...的閒話
example: It's not polite to gossip about other people's private lives behind their backs.
example_cn: 在背後議論別人的私生活是不禮貌的。
simple: They spent the afternoon gossiping about their neighbors.

example: The sculptor used a sharp tool to gouge out the excess clay from the statue.
example_cn: 雕刻家用鋒利的工具鑿掉雕像上多餘的黏土。
simple: He gouged out a hole in the wood.

chinese: 以...增光 / 賞臉 (正式或幽默)
example: We were honored that the famous author decided to grace our event with her presence.
example_cn: 那位知名作家願意蒞臨我們的活動，讓我們深感榮幸。
simple: The ceremony was graced with a performance by the choir.

chinese: 調降等級 / 調升等級
example: The credit agency decided to grade down the country's economic rating due to high debt.
example_cn: 信用評等機構因債務過高，決定調降該國的經濟評級。
simple: Students' assignments were graded up for creativity.

example: The university decided to grant a full scholarship to the most outstanding student.
example_cn: 大學決定給予最優秀的學生全額獎學金。
simple: The right to vote was granted to all citizens.

chinese: 塗抹油脂 / 賄賂 (粗俗)
example: The mechanic needs to grease up the gears to ensure the machine runs smoothly.
example_cn: 技師需要給齒輪上油，以確保機器運轉順暢。
simple: The swimmer greased up his body before entering the cold water.

example: After the first spring rain, the hills began to green up beautifully.
example_cn: 第一場春雨過後，山丘開始變得翠綠，景色很美。
simple: The city has launched a project to green up the urban areas.

chinese: 為...哀悼 / 為...感到悲痛
example: The entire nation began to grieve for the victims of the tragic accident.
example_cn: 全國上下都開始為那場悲劇事故的罹難者哀悼。
simple: She is still grieving for her lost cat.

example: The police grilled the suspect for hours about his whereabouts on the night of the crime.
example_cn: 警方就案發當晚他的行蹤，盤問了嫌犯好幾個小時。
simple: The manager grilled him for the missing files.

example: He has been grinding away at his research project for several months now.
example_cn: 他已經埋頭研究這個計畫好幾個月了。
simple: The constant waves ground away the base of the cliff.

example: He accidentally ground the dirt into the expensive carpet with his heavy boots.
example_cn: 他不小心用厚重的靴子把泥土踩進昂貴的地毯裡。
simple: The wheat was ground into flour.

example: The war continued to grind on, causing suffering to millions of innocent people.
example_cn: 戰爭持續拖下去，讓數百萬無辜百姓受苦。
simple: The meeting ground on for three more hours.

chinese: 以...抓住 / 被...深深震撼
example: The child was gripped with fear when he got lost in the crowded market.
example_cn: 那個小孩在擁擠的市場裡走丟時，嚇得不得了。
pattern: S + be gripped with + N (情感)
simple: He gripped the steering wheel with both hands.

example: The students were groaning about the amount of homework they had to do over the weekend.
example_cn: 學生們都在抱怨週末要寫的作業太多。
simple: Stop groaning about the weather!

example: The fans began to group around the entrance, hoping to see the famous singer.
example_cn: 粉絲們開始聚集在入口處，希望能見到那位知名歌手。
simple: The children grouped around the teacher.

example: The teacher asked the students to group into teams of four for the discussion.
example_cn: 老師要學生分成四人一組進行討論。
simple: These items can be grouped into three categories.

example: He had to grovel to his boss to get his job back after making a major mistake.
example_cn: 他犯了大錯後，得低聲下氣求老闆才能保住工作。
simple: I will not grovel to anyone for a favor.

example: His interest in biology grew from a simple childhood hobby of collecting insects.
example_cn: 他對生物的興趣，是從小時候收集昆蟲這個簡單的嗜好發展而來的。
simple: The small shop grew from a market stall.

example: The young athlete has grown in confidence after winning several important matches.
example_cn: 那位年輕運動員在贏了好幾場重要比賽後，變得更有自信。
simple: The plants grow in a shaded area.

example: The old stone wall was completely grown over with ivy and moss.
example_cn: 那面老舊的石牆完全被常春藤和青苔覆蓋了。
pattern: N + be grown over with + N
simple: The garden path has grown over.

example: He was grubbing about in the drawer, trying to find a spare key.
example_cn: 他在抽屜裡東翻西找，想找一把備用鑰匙。
pattern: S + grub about in + 地方
simple: Pigs were grubbing around in the mud.

chinese: 挖出 / 根除 / (非正式)找出資料
example: The gardener decided to grub up the old, dead bushes to plant new flowers.
example_cn: 園丁決定把枯死的老灌木挖掉，好種新的花。
simple: The detective managed to grub up some facts about the case.

chinese: 湧出 / 噴出 / 滔滔不絕地說
example: Water gushed forth from the broken pipe and flooded the entire basement.
example_cn: 水從破裂的水管裡湧出來，淹沒了整個地下室。
simple: Compliments gushed out when she showed her design.

example: We spent the whole afternoon just hacking around in the park with our friends.
example_cn: 我們整個下午都在公園裡跟朋友閒晃。
simple: They were just hacking around waiting for the show.

chinese: 砍掉 / 使人氣憤 (口語)
example: The gardener had to hack off the dead branches of the tree.
example_cn: 園丁得把樹上枯死的樹枝砍掉。
simple: His rude behavior really hacked me off.

example: The explorers used machetes to hack through the dense jungle vines.
example_cn: 探險家用開山刀砍開叢林裡茂密的藤蔓。
simple: They hacked through the frozen ground.

chinese: 苦苦思索 / 不停地做
example: He has been hammering at this math problem all morning without success.
example_cn: 他整個早上都在死磕這道數學題，還是沒解出來。
simple: Someone was hammering at the door.

chinese: 努力工作 / 持續攻擊 / 反覆強調
example: The journalist kept hammering away at the same question during the interview.
example_cn: 那位記者採訪時一直追問同一個問題。
simple: They hammered away until they reached a deal.

example: Our coach tried to hammer the importance of teamwork into our heads every day.
example_cn: 我們教練每天都不斷灌輸團隊合作的重要性。
simple: He hammered the nail in.

example: Once you finish reading the report, please hand it on to the next person.
example_cn: 報告看完後，請順手傳給下一個人。
simple: Customs were handed on from father to son.

example: A suspicious-looking man was seen hanging about near the school gate.
example_cn: 有人看到一個形跡可疑的男子在校門口附近徘徊。
simple: Stop hanging about and do some work!

example: Everyone else rushed forward, but she hung back, unsure of what to do.
example_cn: 其他人都往前衝，只有她猶豫不前，不曉得該怎麼辦。
simple: He hung back while the others entered the room.

example: Her long black hair hung down almost to her waist.
example_cn: 她那頭黑色長髮幾乎垂到腰際。
simple: Curtains hung down from the windows.

chinese: 堅持住 / 挺住 (非正式)
example: I know the project is difficult, but hang in there! We are almost finished.
example_cn: 我知道這個專案很難，但撐住！我們快完成了。
simple: He hung in until the crisis was over.

chinese: 懸而未決 / 威脅著 / 籠罩
example: The threat of redundancy hung over the factory workers for many months.
example_cn: 裁員的陰影籠罩了工廠員工好幾個月。
simple: A cloud of suspicion hangs over him.

chinese: 偶然出現 / 碰巧路過
example: I was looking for a taxi when an empty one happened along.
example_cn: 我正在找計程車時，剛好有一輛空車經過。
simple: A stranger happened along and helped us.

example: I happened on a very rare book while browsing in a second-hand bookstore.
example_cn: 我在二手書店閒逛時，偶然發現了一本很稀有的書。
simple: They happened on a beautiful beach during their hike.

chinese: 凝固成 / 變硬成為 / 變成 (固定模式)
example: The flowing lava slowly began to cool and harden into solid rock.
example_cn: 流動的熔岩慢慢冷卻，硬化成堅固的岩石。
simple: Small disagreements hardened into deep hatred.

chinese: (使植物)適應室外環境 / 抗寒化
example: You should harden off the seedlings before transplanting them into the garden.
example_cn: 幼苗在移植到花園之前，應該先進行馴化。
simple: The plants need to be hardened off gradually.

chinese: 詳談 / 重新討論 (已討論過的事)
example: There is no need to hash over the same old arguments again and again.
example_cn: 沒必要一再重提那些老掉牙的爭論。
simple: They spent hours hashing over the project details.

chinese: 跳下車 / 跳上車 (大眾運輸)
example: You can hop on the free shuttle bus at the main entrance of the theme park.
example_cn: 你可以在主題樂園的大門口搭免費接駁車。
pattern: S + hop on/off + 大眾運輸
simple: We hopped off the bus at the wrong stop.

example: He told the driver to stop for a moment so he could hop out and buy a newspaper.
example_cn: 他請司機停一下，好讓他跳下車去買份報紙。
simple: She hopped out of bed as soon as the alarm went off.

chinese: 跳過 / 順道拜訪 (非正式)
example: The little boy managed to hop over the small fence into the neighbor's garden.
example_cn: 小男孩成功跳過矮籬笆，溜進鄰居家的花園。
simple: I'll hop over to your house this evening.

chinese: 在萬分渺茫中抱著一線希望
example: They were hoping against hope that the earthquake survivors would still be found alive.
example_cn: 他們抱著一絲希望，期盼地震生還者還能被找到。
pattern: S + be hoping against hope that + 子句
simple: She hoped against hope for a miracle.

example: The firefighters spent an hour hosing down the building to cool the hot walls.
example_cn: 消防隊員花了一小時用水柱沖洗建築物，冷卻高溫的牆壁。
simple: He hosed down the dusty driveway.

chinese: 驅逐 / 迫使 (某人) 離開 (職位或地方)
example: The official was hounded out of office after the details of the scandal were revealed.
example_cn: 那官員在醜聞細節曝光後，被逼得辭職下台。
pattern: S + be hounded out of + N
simple: The media hounded him out of his home.

example: The stray dogs were housed in a large temporary shelter near the city outskirts.
example_cn: 那些流浪狗被安置在郊區附近一個大型的臨時收容所裡。
simple: The library collection is housed in a fireproof vault.

chinese: 盤旋 / 徘徊 / 守在旁邊
example: The waiter continued to hover around our table, waiting for us to finish our meal.
example_cn: 那個服務生一直在我們桌子旁邊轉來轉去，等著我們吃完飯。
simple: A hawk was hovering over the field.

chinese: 吼叫使某人無法說話 / 喝倒采
example: The protesters tried to howl down the speaker, so no one could hear his speech.
example_cn: 抗議者試圖用噓聲蓋過演講者的聲音，讓大家都聽不到他說話。
simple: The crowd howled down the unpopular politician.

chinese: 擠在一起 / 縮成一團 / 緊挨著
example: The penguins huddled together against the freezing Antarctic winds to stay warm.
example_cn: 企鵝們擠在一起，抵擋南極的寒風，好讓自己保持溫暖。
simple: The kittens were huddling up for warmth.

example: I spent the whole morning trying to hunt out my old high school yearbooks.
example_cn: 我花了一整個早上，想把我高中的舊畢業紀念冊找出來。
simple: We need to hunt up some more evidence.

example: The angry man hurled a stone through the window of the abandoned house.
example_cn: 那個生氣的男人朝那間廢棄屋子的窗戶扔了一塊石頭。
simple: He hurled the ball into the net.

phrase: hurry away / back / in / into / off / on / through
chinese: 趕緊離開 / 趕回 / 匆忙進入
example: Don't hurry through your breakfast; you still have plenty of time before school.
example_cn: 早餐不用急著吃，離上學還有很多時間。
simple: She hurried back home when she realized she forgot her keys.

chinese: 急需 / 缺乏 (通常用於否定)
example: That successful company is certainly not hurting for new investment opportunities.
example_cn: 那家成功的公司當然不缺新的投資機會。
simple: He is not hurting for money these days.

chinese: 疾馳而下 / 飛速穿過
example: The race car hurtled through the finish line at an incredible speed.
example_cn: 那輛賽車以驚人的速度衝過終點線。
simple: A huge rock hurtled down the mountainside.

chinese: 催促進入 / 誘使 (某人) 匆忙做...
example: The suspicious salesman tried to hustle me into signing the contract without reading it.
example_cn: 那個可疑的業務員一直催我，想讓我在沒看合約的情況下就簽字。
simple: The guards hustled the prisoners into the van.

example: The lake usually ices over in January, allowing local residents to go skating.
example_cn: 那座湖通常在一月結冰，讓當地居民可以去溜冰。
simple: The car windshield iced up during the night.

example: Stop idling around and start focusing on your project; the deadline is tomorrow.
example_cn: 別再閒晃了，趕快專心在你的專案上，明天就是截止日了。
simple: They spent the afternoon idling about in the park.

chinese: 引發成為 / 點燃變成
example: The small disagreement suddenly ignited into a fierce argument between the two neighbors.
example_cn: 那場小爭執突然在兩個鄰居之間演變成一場激烈的爭吵。
simple: The dry grass ignited into a major fire.

example: The president was impeached for abuse of power and obstruction of justice.
example_cn: 那位總統因為濫用職權和妨礙司法而被彈劾。
simple: The judge was impeached for bribery.

example: Lack of proper funding has impeded the scientists from continuing their research.
example_cn: 缺乏足夠的經費，阻礙了科學家們繼續他們的研究。
simple: The injury impeded him from running.

example: The prisoners implored for mercy, but the cruel dictator ignored their pleas.
example_cn: 囚犯們懇求慈悲，但那個殘酷的獨裁者對他們的哀求置之不理。
simple: She implored her parents for a second chance.

example: The company imports high-tech components from Japan to assemble its products.
example_cn: 那家公司從日本進口高科技零件來組裝他們的產品。
pattern: S + import + N + from + 來源
simple: Oil is imported into the country from abroad.

example: The teacher tried to impress on the students the importance of regular exercise.
example_cn: 老師試圖讓學生深刻體會到規律運動的重要性。
simple: He impressed upon me the need for secrecy.

example: She impressed the interviewers with her extensive knowledge of the industry.
example_cn: 她以對該行業的豐富知識，給面試官留下了深刻的印象。
simple: The building impressed everyone with its size.

example: The revolutionary was imprisoned in a small, dark cell for many years.
example_cn: 那位革命人士被關在一間又小又暗的牢房裡許多年。
simple: She felt imprisoned in an unhappy marriage.

chinese: 在...上即興創作 / 即興發揮
example: The jazz musician began to improvise on the main melody of the song.
example_cn: 這位爵士樂手開始在歌曲的主旋律上即興發揮。
simple: He had to improvise on his speech when he lost his notes.

chinese: 以...正式開展 / 以...舉行開幕式
example: The new museum was inaugurated with a special exhibition of ancient art.
example_cn: 這間新博物館以一場古代藝術特展揭幕啟用。
simple: The bridge was inaugurated with a grand ceremony.

example: The politician was accused of trying to incite the crowd to violence.
example_cn: 這位政治人物被指控試圖煽動群眾使用暴力。
simple: The speech incited the soldiers to rebellion.

example: I incline to the view that education is the key to solving most social problems.
example_cn: 我傾向於認為教育是解決大多數社會問題的關鍵。
simple: He inclines toward a more traditional style.

chinese: 併入 / 融入 / 納入
example: The designers decided to incorporate traditional elements into the modern building.
example_cn: 設計師決定將傳統元素融入這棟現代建築中。
simple: The suggestion was incorporated into the final report.

example: The eggs are carefully incubated in a temperature-controlled environment for 21 days.
example_cn: 這些雞蛋在恆溫控制的環境中仔細孵化二十一天。
pattern: S + be incubated in + 地方
simple: The idea was incubated in the small research lab.

example: The manager has already indented for a new set of office furniture.
example_cn: 經理已經下訂單要一套新的辦公家具。
simple: We need to indent for more stationery.

chinese: 簽約成為...的學徒 / 束縛
example: In the past, many young boys were indentured to master craftsmen to learn a trade.
example_cn: 過去，很多年輕男孩被簽約當學徒，跟隨師傅學習手藝。
simple: He was indentured to a printer.

example: The scientist indicated to the committee that the initial results were promising.
example_cn: 這位科學家向委員會表示初步結果相當有希望。
simple: She indicated the direction to the tourist.

example: The former senator was officially indicted for tax fraud and conspiracy.
example_cn: 這位前參議員因稅務詐欺和共謀罪被正式起訴。
simple: The gang was indicted for murder.

chinese: 大批滋生 / 遍佈 (害蟲)
example: The old wooden house was found to be infested with termites and needed treatment.
example_cn: 這棟老舊木屋被發現有白蟻侵蝕，需要進行處理。
simple: The kitchen was infested with cockroaches.

example: Spies managed to infiltrate into the enemy's headquarters during the night.
example_cn: 間諜在夜間成功滲透進入敵方總部。
simple: New ideas slowly infiltrate into the community.

example: His speech was designed to inflame the crowd with anger and resentment.
example_cn: 他的演講刻意要激起群眾的憤怒與不滿。
simple: His eyes were inflamed with dust.

example: He used a small pump to inflate the basketball with air before the game.
example_cn: 他在比賽前用一個小打氣筒把籃球充飽氣。
simple: He was inflated with pride after winning.

chinese: 告發 / 舉報 (某人的罪行)
example: The criminal decided to inform on his partners to get a shorter sentence.
example_cn: 這名罪犯決定出賣同夥以換取較短的刑期。
simple: She refused to inform against her friends.

example: She was infuriated with his constant lies and decided to break up with him.
example_cn: 她對他持續說謊感到非常惱火，決定跟他分手。
simple: His arrogance infuriated me.

chinese: 注入 (品質、情感) / 泡 (茶)
example: The new manager tried to infuse some enthusiasm into the discouraged team.
example_cn: 新任經理試圖為士氣低落的團隊注入一些熱情。
simple: The herbs should be infused into hot water.

example: Please initial the bottom of each page with your pen to confirm you've read it.
example_cn: 請用筆在每一頁下方簽上姓名縮寫，確認你已經讀過。
simple: The changes were initialed with blue ink.

example: He was initiated into the secret society during a ceremony held last night.
example_cn: 他在昨晚的一場儀式中被正式引入這個秘密社團。
simple: She initiated me into the mysteries of gardening.

chinese: 注射進 / 注入 (金錢、新活力)
example: The government decided to inject more money into the public school system.
example_cn: 政府決定投入更多經費到公立學校系統中。
simple: His presence injected some humor into the meeting.

example: The expensive jewelry box was beautifully inset with small diamonds.
example_cn: 這個昂貴的珠寶盒上鑲嵌著漂亮的小鑽石。
simple: The table was inset with a marble pattern.

example: Parents should strive to instill a sense of honesty in their children.
example_cn: 父母應該努力在孩子心中灌輸誠實的觀念。
simple: The coach instilled confidence in his players.

example: Parents should strive to instill a strong sense of responsibility into their children from a young age.
example_cn: 父母應該從小就努力培養孩子強烈的責任感。
simple: Good teachers instill confidence into their students.

example: The company decided to institute a new policy with specific guidelines for remote work.
example_cn: 公司決定針對遠距工作制定一套有明確規範的新政策。
simple: The law was instituted with much public support.

example: The competition for the championship will likely intensify with the arrival of the new season.
example_cn: 隨著新賽季的到來，冠軍爭奪戰很可能會更加激烈。
simple: His headache intensified with the loud noise.

example: The lawyer decided to intercede for the young defendant, hoping for a lighter sentence.
example_cn: 律師決定為年輕的被告出面說情，希望能減輕刑罰。
simple: Friends interceded for him to get the job back.

chinese: 向 (某人) 求情 / 調解
example: I asked the manager to intercede with the board of directors on my behalf.
example_cn: 我請經理幫我向董事會說情。
simple: He interceded with the authorities for the prisoner.

example: Intelligence agencies managed to intercept secret messages from the enemy group.
example_cn: 情報機構成功攔截了敵方組織的祕密訊息。
pattern: S + intercept + N + from + 來源
simple: The letter was intercepted from the mail.

example: In some contexts, the two technical terms can be interchanged with each other.
example_cn: 在某些情況下，這兩個專業術語可以互換使用。
simple: Daylight interchanges with darkness.

example: The railway network is designed to interconnect with the major airports in the country.
example_cn: 這個鐵路網的設計是為了與國內主要機場相互連接。
simple: The systems are interconnected with high-speed cables.

chinese: 禁止 (某人) 做某事 / 禁制
example: The pilot was interdicted from flying for six months due to a safety violation.
example_cn: 這名飛行員因違反安全規定被禁止飛行六個月。
simple: They were interdicted from entering the building.

type: 動詞 / 名詞 + 介系詞
example: Traveling can interest people in learning about diverse cultures and histories.
example_cn: 旅行可以引發人們對多元文化與歷史的興趣。
simple: She has an interest in ancient history.

example: He occasionally interjected comments into the discussion to express his doubts.
example_cn: 他不時在討論中插話，表達自己的疑問。
simple: He interjected a question into the speech.

example: The novel's plot is interlaced with several mysterious subplots.
example_cn: 這部小說的劇情交織著好幾個神祕的支線情節。
simple: Fingers were interlaced with each other.

example: The book contains beautiful illustrations interleaved with the text.
example_cn: 這本書在文字之間穿插了精美的插圖。
simple: Lessons are interleaved with practical exercises.

example: Our individual successes are closely interlinked with the performance of the team.
example_cn: 我們個人的成功與團隊的表現緊密相連。
simple: The history of the two nations is interlinked.

example: The pieces of the puzzle are designed to interlock with each other perfectly.
example_cn: 拼圖的每一片都設計成能完美地互相扣合。
simple: The two systems interlock to provide security.

chinese: 闖入 / 干涉 (他人事務)
example: He was accused of trying to interlope in the company's internal affairs.
example_cn: 他被指控試圖干涉公司的內部事務。
simple: Don't interlope in their argument.

example: Over many centuries, the different ethnic groups began to intermarry with each other.
example_cn: 經過好幾個世紀，不同的族群開始互相通婚。
simple: Royal families often intermarried with each other.

example: Fact and fiction often intermingle with each other in historical novels.
example_cn: 在歷史小說中，事實與虛構經常交織在一起。
simple: The tourists intermingled with the locals.

example: The chemicals should not be allowed to intermix with water during the experiment.
example_cn: 實驗過程中不應該讓這些化學藥劑混到水裡。
simple: Oil does not intermix with water.

example: He spent the summer interning in the marketing department of a tech giant.
example_cn: 他暑假在一家科技巨頭的市場部門實習。
simple: She was interned in a camp during the war.

example: The small local business eventually internationalized into a famous global brand.
example_cn: 這家在地小企業最終國際化，成了知名的全球品牌。
simple: The curriculum was internationalized into a diverse program.

example: The various elements of the culture interpenetrate with each other to form a unique society.
example_cn: 文化中的各種元素彼此交融，形成一個獨特的社會。
simple: Religious and political ideas often interpenetrate.

example: Some later editors chose to interpolate their own opinions into the original text.
example_cn: 有些後來的編輯選擇在原文中插入自己的意見。
simple: Data points were interpolated into the graph.

example: He tried to interpose himself between the two arguing neighbors to stop the fight.
example_cn: 他試圖介入兩個正在爭吵的鄰居之間，阻止他們打架。
simple: A barrier was interposed between the two groups.

example: Psychologists study how different emotions interrelate with each other in human behavior.
example_cn: 心理學家研究人類行為中，不同情緒如何相互關聯。
simple: Poverty and crime are often interrelated.

example: The police interrogated the suspect for hours about his whereabouts on the night of the crime.
example_cn: 警方審問嫌犯好幾個小時，問他案發當晚的去向。
simple: The witness was interrogated about her testimony.

example: The speaker was frequently interrupted with loud cheers and applause from the audience.
example_cn: 演講者頻頻被觀眾的熱情歡呼與掌聲打斷。
simple: She interrupted him with a question.

example: The new highway is planned to intersect with the existing railway line near the city.
example_cn: 這條新高速公路預計在市區附近與現有的鐵路交會。
simple: Where the two roads intersect, there is a gas station.

chinese: 散佈在...之間 / 以...點綴
